exclusion (fail build if any timeline event matches any of
N excluded kinds), compose with --actor-id-not from ADR-0207
("events NOT by automation actors AND NOT of maintenance
kinds" cohort exclusion across timeline). ADDITIVE adapter
fields — all 3 diff-timeline input types
(DiffHistoryTimelineInput + DiffHistoryTimelineNwayInput +
DiffHistoryTimelineCrossTableInput) gain eventKindsNot?:
ReadonlyArray<OptOutHistoryEventKind> multi-value from
inception; no breaking rename; existing consumers unaffected.
Adapter WHERE clause 3-path symmetry — each of 3 adapter
methods gains the same conditional WHERE block positioned
immediately after the existing eventKinds IN block;
parameterized NOT IN clause; matches established ADR-0193/
0194/0199/0200/0202/0207/0213 3-dispatch-path symmetry
pattern (every diff-timeline filter exists in 3 identical
conditional WHERE clauses). event_kind never null by
constraint so no IS NULL handling needed. CLI dispatcher
runRetentionDiffTimeline parses kindNotFlags via getMultiFlag
ONCE at top of function before path dispatch with per-

matching ADR-0200/0214/0217/0220/0221/0222 pattern (exits 2
on FIRST invalid value with that value named); then threaded
to whichever of 3 adapter methods is selected. JSON envelope
3 shapes (pair-wise no-discriminator + nway:true +
crossTable:true) all gain kindsNot: string[] | null field
positioned after kinds matching array-or-null canonical
multi-value envelope shape. cli.ts helpText updated — both

[--kind-not <event-kind> ...] indicating repeatable; pair-
wise description extended with "--kind-not is repeatable and
EXCLUDES entries with any of the listed event_kinds (OR-
semantic NOT IN; mirror of --kind)"; cross-table description
extended to enumerate --kind-not alongside other repeatable
flags. Composition with --kind — both clauses fire
independently at PG layer across all 3 paths; --kind X --kind
Y --kind-not Z produces h.event_kind IN ($X, $Y) AND
h.event_kind NOT IN ($Z); matches ADR-0222 composition
behavior on history surface. Check ordering preserved — NOT
IN fires AFTER IN within WHERE conditions (positive before
negative). Adapter test block NEW "PostgresTraceRetention
diff-timeline --kind-not exclusion filter (M6.7.zz.tenant
.opt-out.cli.diff-timeline.kind-not.multi)" describe block —
pair-wise single-value NOT IN ($N) (NEW), pair-wise multi-

NOT IN independently (NEW), pair-wise omits NOT IN when
empty array (NEW), N-way threads via --add-tenant dispatch
(NEW), cross-table threads via --cross-table dispatch (NEW).

describe block — exit 2 on invalid --kind-not, exit 2 on

(NEW), pair-wise composes with --kind, N-way threads via

(NEW), JSON envelope echoes kindsNot array pair-wise (NEW),

The retention CLI's multi-value `--kind-not` coverage now
spans ALL 3 retention surfaces — history (ADR-0222) + diff-

history (ADR-0214). The kind dimension symmetry on diff-

M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.history.kind-not.multi
closes ADR-0206 Q4 by adding NEW `--kind-not <event-kind>`
repeatable flag on `retention history` for multi-value OR-
semantic substrate-side NOT IN exclusion filter. Operator
excludes audit log entries with `event_kind IN {N excluded
event_kinds}`. 12th milestone in the multi-value family but
ADDITIVE not breaking — --kind-not doesn't exist on retention
history yet so this is a NEW field multi-value from
inception (not a rename); pattern matches ADR-0211 history
actor-id-not which was also added as ADDITIVE multi-value
from inception. Restores within-surface symmetry on the kind
dimension on retention history — positive --kind (ADR-0221
just-shipped) + negative --kind-not (this ADR) now symmetric;
matches diff-history (ADR-0217 + ADR-0214) symmetry. Real
cohort-negative-exclusion use cases on history surface —
workflow audit excluding maintenance kinds (events EXCLUDING
policy_deleted + retention_set focusing audit on opt-out
workflow without maintenance noise), anti-deletion forensic
(events EXCLUDING policy_deleted surfacing only non-
destructive mutations during incident window), cohort-aware
audit with positive + negative (--kind opt_out_set
opt_out_cleared --kind-not policy_deleted), CI gate with
kind-list exclusion (fail build if any history event matches
any of N excluded kinds), compose with --actor-id-not
(already multi from ADR-0211) — "events NOT by automation
actors AND NOT of maintenance kinds" cohort exclusion.
ADDITIVE adapter field ListOptOutHistoryInput.eventKindsNot?:
ReadonlyArray<OptOutHistoryEventKind> — multi-value from
inception; no breaking rename; existing consumers unaffected.
Adapter WHERE clause parameterized NOT IN with multi-value
placeholders matching ADR-0211 history actor-id-not pattern
exactly (substituting event_kind for actor_id); event_kind
is NEVER NULL by constraint so no IS NULL handling needed
unlike actor-id-not's `IS NULL OR NOT IN (...)` pattern.
CLI runRetentionHistory reads via getMultiFlag("kind-not")
with per-occurrence validation via isOptOutHistoryEventKind
loop matching ADR-0200/0214/0217/0220/0221 pattern (exits 2
on FIRST invalid value with that value named). JSON envelope
new field eventKindsNot: string[] | null matching array-or-
null canonical multi-value envelope shape across the family.
cli.ts helpText added new line [--kind-not <event-kind> ...]
to retention history usage line + description added "--kind-
not is repeatable and EXCLUDES entries with any of the
listed event_kinds (OR-semantic NOT IN; mirror of --kind)".
Composition with --kind — both clauses fire independently at
PG layer; --kind X --kind Y --kind-not Z produces SQL
h.event_kind IN ($X, $Y) AND h.event_kind NOT IN ($Z);
redundant combinations (--kind X --kind-not X) produce empty
result PG handles naturally CLI doesn't surface
contradiction error matching ADR-0211 actor-id + actor-id-
not behavior. Adapter test block extended — 4 new tests:
excludes by single eventKindsNot (verifies NOT IN ($1) +
params), excludes by multiple with NOT IN clause (NEW
multi-value verifying NOT IN ($1, $2) + 2 params), omits
NOT IN clause when eventKindsNot empty array (NEW empty-
array-as-filter-not-set), composes eventKinds + eventKindsNot
independently (NEW both clauses fire). CLI test block
extended — 6 new tests: exit 2 on invalid --kind-not value,
threads single-element array when --kind-not set once,
threads multi-element array when --kind-not repeated (NEW),
composes --kind + --kind-not threading both independently
(NEW), JSON envelope echoes eventKindsNot array when set,
JSON envelope eventKindsNot=null when not set. Test count:
9,113 → 9,123 (+10 net: adapter +4, CLI +6). ADR-0222
documents the design + 10 rejected alternatives + 6 future
Qs (--kind-not on retention diff-timeline pairs with ADR-
0200 as negative symmetric across 3 dispatch paths defer
natural follow-up, --kind-not @file.txt defer 4-value enum
bounded, CLI-side dedup defer PG handles, semantic-shape
exclusion grouping --exclude-maintenance-kinds shorthand
defer operator-policy, cross-flag contradiction detection
defer current PG-returns-empty observably correct, compose
with multi-value --actor-id-not verify in tests). The
retention CLI's multi-value `--kind-not` coverage now spans
2 of 3 retention surfaces — history (this milestone) + diff-
history (ADR-0214); diff-timeline --kind-not is the natural
follow-up. The kind dimension on retention history is the
2nd dimension to receive complete positive + negative multi-
value symmetry on its surface (matches diff-history's
ADR-0217 + ADR-0214). Natural follow-up milestones —
--kind-not on retention diff-timeline (ADR-0200 family Q —
completes kind dimension symmetry on 3rd retention surface),
JSON envelope shape documentation as family-wide ADR (across
12 multi-value milestones), cross-flag contradiction
detection across 3 retention surfaces.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.history.kind.multi
closes ADR-0206 Q1 by widening `--kind <event-kind>` from
single-value to repeated flag (OR-semantic substrate-side IN
filter) on `retention history` using the multiFlags
infrastructure from ADR-0183. Operator filters the audit log
to entries with `event_kind IN {N event_kinds}`. 11th
milestone in the actor + kind multi-value family extending
multi-value to the third retention surface (retention history
substrate-side filter; diff-timeline + diff-history already
covered). Different semantic from diff-history --kind
(ADR-0217 per-event-pair expectation check) — retention
history --kind is list-style WHERE filter returning ONLY
events whose event_kind is in the tuple; matches ADR-0200
diff-timeline --kind semantic. Real cohort-positive-filter
use cases on history surface — workflow audit query (list
events with kind IN (opt_out_set, opt_out_cleared) focusing
audit on opt-out workflow without retention/policy noise),
mutation cohort report (events with kind IN (opt_out_set,
retention_set) focusing on policy mutations), CI gate
aggregation (count events by kind tuple over window),
forensic timeline filter (compose with --actor-id already
multi from ADR-0210 for cohort analysis), operator dashboard
backing query (UI lists "deletion-class events" vs "workflow
events"). Breaking adapter rename ListOptOutHistoryInput
.eventKind?: OptOutHistoryEventKind → eventKinds?:
ReadonlyArray<OptOutHistoryEventKind>; session-recent code
from ADR-0206 with no external consumers contained scope
matches established one-shot break precedent across 10 prior
single→multi renames. Adapter WHERE clause rewritten with
parameterized IN clause and multi-value placeholders matching
ADR-0210/0211 history actor multi-value pattern exactly —
same param.push loop + same IN (...) clause shape + same
empty-array-as-filter-not-set convention. CLI
runRetentionHistory reads via getMultiFlag("kind") with per-
occurrence validation via isOptOutHistoryEventKind loop
matching ADR-0200/0214/0217/0220 pattern (exits 2 on FIRST
invalid value with that value named). JSON envelope renamed
eventKind: string | null → eventKinds: string[] | null
matching ADR-0207/0210/0211/0214/0217/0218/0219/0220 array-
or-null canonical multi-value envelope shape across the
family. cli.ts helpText updated from [--kind <event-kind>]
to [--kind <event-kind> ...] indicating repeatable + global
flag description note added "Repeatable for multi-value OR-
semantic IN filter". Adapter test block extended — original
single-value test renamed to "filters by single eventKind
when provided (multi-value array)", added "filters by
multiple eventKinds with IN clause when provided (OR-
semantic)" verifying 2-element IN ($1, $2) clause + 2
params (NEW), "omits the event_kind WHERE clause when
eventKinds is empty array" verifying empty array behavior
(NEW), "omits the event_kind WHERE clause when eventKinds
not set (backward compat)" preserving original semantic
checking for IN substring specifically (NEW); 2 composition
tests updated to expect h.event_kind IN ($3) instead of =
$3. CLI test block extended — exit 2 on invalid --kind, exit
2 on FIRST invalid --kind occurrence when multi (NEW),
threads multi-element eventKinds array when --kind repeated
(NEW), JSON envelope echoes multi-element eventKinds array
when --kind repeated (NEW), JSON envelope eventKinds=null
when --kind NOT set (NEW). Test count: 9,106 → 9,113 (+7
net: adapter +3, CLI +4). ADR-0221 documents the design +
10 rejected alternatives + 6 future Qs (--kind-not on
retention history closes ADR-0206 Q4 deferred natural
follow-up, --kind @file.txt defer 4-value enum bounded,
CLI-side dedup of duplicates defer PG handles fine, semantic-
shape grouping --kind=opt-out-workflow shorthand defer
operator-policy, compose with multi-value --kind-not on
history defer until --kind-not exists, per-tenant --kind
filter defer operator-policy). The retention CLI's multi-
value `--kind` flag coverage now spans ALL 3 retention
surfaces — history (this milestone, list-style filter),
diff-timeline (ADR-0200, list-style filter across 3
dispatch paths), diff-history (ADR-0217, per-event-pair
expectation check). Surface-appropriate semantics preserved
across the family. Natural follow-up milestones — --kind-not
on retention history (ADR-0206 Q4 — pairs with this ADR as
negative symmetric, completes within-surface symmetry on
history kind dimension), JSON envelope shape documentation
as family-wide ADR (consistency across 11 multi-value
milestones), cross-flag contradiction detection across 3
retention surfaces.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.per-side.multi
closes ADR-0215 deferred per-side multi-value future Qs in a
bulk milestone — widens 6 per-side flag pairs from single-
value to repeated flags (OR-semantic tuple expectations per
side): --kind-a / --kind-b (closes ADR-0217 family Q),
--actor-id-a / --actor-id-b (closes ADR-0218 family Q),
--actor-id-not-a / --actor-id-not-b (closes ADR-0219 family
Q). 10th milestone in the actor + kind multi-value family
joining 9 prior global widenings (ADR-0199/0200/0207/0210/
0211/0214/0217/0218/0219); with this milestone per-side
variants reach multi-value parity with global on diff-history.
All per-side expectation-check dimensions on diff-history now
multi-value — --kind-a/b + --kind-not-a/b (already multi from
ADR-0215) + --actor-id-a/b + --actor-id-not-a/b. Only
--system-only-a/b + --no-system-a/b remain single-value
(boolean by nature). Bulk approach justified — each rename is
mechanical (9 prior precedents make pattern routine),
splitting into 3 separate milestones would triple CLAUDE.md/
README/index/commit churn without architectural benefit, and
operators using one per-side multi-value variant typically
want all three. Real per-side multi-value use cases — per-
side cohort positive expectation (assert A is in cohort
{Alice, Bob} AND B is in cohort {Carol, Dave}), per-side
exclusion tuple (assert A is NOT in migration SA list of 3
AND B unconstrained one-sided forensic), per-side kind tuple
expectation (assert A is in opt-out workflow kinds {opt_out_
set, opt_out_cleared} AND B is a deletion kind {policy_
deleted}), compose with global multi-value (global --actor-
id {Alice, Bob, Carol} requires BOTH in trio + per-side
--actor-id-a {Alice, Bob} further constrains A), hybrid
global + per-side belt-and-suspenders. Breaking adapter
renames (6 fields) — eventKindA?: OptOutHistoryEventKind →
eventKindsA?: ReadonlyArray<OptOutHistoryEventKind>,
eventKindB → eventKindsB, actorIdA?: string → actorIdsA?:
ReadonlyArray<string>, actorIdB → actorIdsB, actorIdNotA →
actorIdsNotA, actorIdNotB → actorIdsNotB; matches established
one-shot break precedent across 9 prior single→multi renames.
All 6 check blocks rewritten with Set-based O(1) membership
lookup; <system> placeholder for null actor_id preserved
across positive variants; always-list error format `in ['X']`
for single + `in ['X', 'Y']` for multi (matches ADR-0217/
0218/0219 family). CLI parsing — all 6 flags switched from
getStringFlag to getMultiFlag; per-occurrence validation via
isOptOutHistoryEventKind loop for kind flags (matches
ADR-0200/0214/0217 pattern); no validation for actor flags
(UUIDs free-form). JSON envelope shape — 6 field renames
kindA/kindB/actorIdA/actorIdB/actorIdNotA/actorIdNotB →
kindsA/kindsB/actorIdsA/actorIdsB/actorIdsNotA/actorIdsNotB
matching array-or-null canonical multi-value envelope shape.
cli.ts helpText updated — all 6 per-side flags from <uuid> /
<event-kind> to <uuid> ... / <event-kind> ... indicating
repeatable + description blocks extended explaining
"repeatable + side A / side B has any of the listed actors /
event_kinds (OR-semantic tuple) + per-side fires
independently". Check ordering preserved — global fires first
then per-side; within per-side A before B; across dimensions
kind → actor → actorPresence. Adapter test block rewritten +
expanded under renamed "diffHistoryEntries per-side
expectation checks (M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history
.per-side + .multi)" describe block — single-value kind-a/b
accepts + throws with new in [...] format (2 each per side),
multi-value kind-a/b accepts + throws when none match (2 each
per side), single-value actor-id-a/b accepts + throws with
new format + <system> rendering, multi-value actor-id-a/b
accepts + throws, single-value actor-id-not-a/b accepts +
throws with new NOT in [...] format, multi-value
actor-id-not-a/b accepts + throws, all-4-per-side multi-value
composition test, global-fires-first composition test, treats
all 6 per-side empty arrays as filter-not-set. CLI test block
rewritten + expanded under renamed "runRetention diff-history
per-side expectations (M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history
.per-side + .multi)" describe block — invalid value exits 2
on each per-side flag, per-occurrence FIRST-invalid-fail-fast
on --kind-a + --kind-not-a, threads single-element + multi-
element arrays on each per-side flag, omits all per-side
fields backward compat, composes with global + per-side as
arrays, JSON envelope echoes all per-side array shapes,
adapter per-side error propagates exit 1 with multi-value
list error format. Test count: 9,091 → 9,106 (+15 net:
adapter +9, CLI +6). ADR-0220 documents the bulk-milestone
design + 10 rejected alternatives + 6 future Qs (per-side
per-occurrence UUID validation defer adapter handles
naturally, per-side @file.txt source defer per-side cohorts
typically smaller, combined cross-per-side contradiction
detection defer current ordering handles naturally, CLI-side
dedup defer Set handles fine, per-side multi-value on diff-
timeline / history N/A already multi where applicable, per-
side semantic shape grouping --A-opt-out-workflow shorthand
defer operator-policy). The retention CLI's diff-history
surface now has the COMPLETE multi-value matrix — global
symmetric (--kind/--actor-id/--actor-id-not/--kind-not all
multi) + per-side asymmetric (--kind-a/b + --actor-id-a/b +
--actor-id-not-a/b + --kind-not-a/b all multi) + actor-
presence (--system-only/--no-system + per-side variants
single-value boolean). Operators have unprecedented forensic
precision — multi-value tuple expressions on both global
symmetric + per-side asymmetric, across both positive +
negative, on both kind + actor dimensions, with system-
presence boolean dimension orthogonal. Effectively any state-
transition assertion expressible in a single command. Natural
follow-up milestones — multi-value --kind on retention
history (ADR-0206 Q1 different surface, substrate-side
filter), JSON envelope shape documentation as family-wide
ADR (consistency across 10 multi-value milestones), cross-
flag contradiction detection (ergonomic improvement across
3 retention surfaces).
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.actor-id-not.multi
closes ADR-0205 Q1 by widening `--actor-id-not <uuid>` from
single-value to repeated flag (OR-semantic tuple exclusion
check) on `retention diff-history` using the multiFlags
infrastructure from ADR-0183. Operator declares "I expect
NEITHER event to have actor_id in {set of N excluded UUIDs}"
and adapter throws on match with side(s) named. Completes the
within-action symmetry on the actor dimension on diff-history —
both positive --actor-id (ADR-0218 just-shipped) and negative
--actor-id-not are now multi-value tuple expressions; the kind
dimension already has this symmetry (--kind from ADR-0217 +
--kind-not from ADR-0214). 9th consecutive single→multi rename
across the actor + kind family (ADR-0199/0200/0207/0210/0211/
0214/0217/0218/0219). The diff-history global multi-value
matrix is now COMPLETE — only --system-only/--no-system remain
single-value (boolean by nature). Real cohort-negative-
exclusion use cases on actor dimension — multi-actor exclusion
forensic (NEITHER event in suspect actor cohort of 3
compromised SA candidates during incident investigation),
workflow audit excluding migration cohort (NEITHER event from
migration SA 1 OR migration SA 2 OR backfill SA), CI gate with
actor-tuple exclusion (fail build if either event authored by
any of 3 excluded automation accounts), anti-automation pair
verification (NEITHER event from automation accounts pre-list),
compose with multi-value --actor-id positive (canonical "events
by Alice OR Bob but NOT Carol OR Dave" cohort positive + tuple
exclusion), compose with --kind multi-value exclusion (NEITHER
event is policy_deleted OR retention_set AND NEITHER event
authored by excluded SA list). Breaking adapter rename
DiffHistoryEntriesInput.actorIdNot?: string → actorIdsNot?:
ReadonlyArray<string>; session-recent code from ADR-0205 with
no external consumers contained scope matches established one-
shot break precedent. Adapter rewritten with Set-based O(1)
membership lookup; null actor_id never matches any UUID in
exclusion tuple by construction (system events pass exclusion —
operators wanting "exclude system" use --no-system instead);
always-list error format `actor_id in ['X']` for single-value +
`actor_id in ['X', 'Y']` for multi-value (single-value error
breaks from ADR-0205's `actor_id 'X'` shape — acceptable since
field rename is itself breaking; consistent rendering across
single + multi mirrors ADR-0218 positive-side format restoring
symmetry across positive + negative). CLI
runRetentionDiffHistory reads via getMultiFlag("actor-id-not")
instead of getStringFlag; no per-occurrence validation needed
(UUIDs are free-form strings at CLI boundary matching ADR-0207/
0210/0211/0218 pattern). JSON envelope renamed actorIdNot:
string | null → actorIdsNot: string[] | null matching ADR-0207/
0210/0211/0214/0217/0218 array-or-null canonical multi-value
envelope shape across the family. Per-side --actor-id-not-a /
--actor-id-not-b stay single-value (deferred future Q for
ADR-0215 family). cli.ts helpText updated from [--actor-id-not
<uuid>] to [--actor-id-not <uuid> ...] indicating repeatable +
description extended explaining "repeatable + NEITHER event
authored by any of the listed actors (OR-semantic exclusion) +
match by either side on any listed actor exits 1 (anti-actor
verification, mirror of --actor-id)". Adapter test block
rewritten + expanded from 8 to 12 tests under renamed
"diffHistoryEntries --actor-id-not exclusion check (M6.7.zz
.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.actor-not + .multi)" describe
block — single-value accepts neither matches, single-value
accepts both system, single-value throws A matches with new
actor_id in ['X'] format, single-value throws B matches,
single-value throws naming both, multi-value accepts neither
has any of N excluded actors (NEW), multi-value throws A
matches one of N with multi-value error format (NEW), multi-
value throws naming both when both events match different
actors in exclusion list (NEW), omits check when actorIdsNot
not set backward compat, treats empty actorIdsNot array as
filter-not-set (NEW), composes with --actor-id positive
expectation check, contradictory --actor-id + --actor-id-not
surfaces actorId check first. CLI test block rewritten +
expanded from 6 to 10 tests under renamed "runRetention diff-
history --actor-id-not (M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history
.actor-not + .multi)" describe block — threads single-element
array when --actor-id-not set once, threads multi-element
array when --actor-id-not repeated (NEW), omits when not set
backward compat, composes with --actor-id, composes with
multi-value --actor-id and multi-value --actor-id-not (NEW),
adapter exclusion error propagates exit 1 with multi-value
error format, JSON envelope echoes single-element array, JSON
envelope echoes multi-element array (NEW), JSON envelope
actorIdsNot=null when not set. Test count: 9,083 → 9,091 (+8
net: adapter +4, CLI +4). ADR-0219 documents the design + 10
rejected alternatives + 6 future Qs (per-side multi-value
--actor-id-not-a / --actor-id-not-b defer, --actor-id-not
@file.txt defer UUID lists scripted via xargs, combined
positive/negative tuple ordering disambiguation defer current
ordering canonical, multi-value --actor-id-not on retention
history N/A already from ADR-0211, CLI-side dedup defer Set
handles fine, semantic-shape exclusion grouping --exclude-
automation-actors shorthand defer operator-policy). The
retention CLI's diff-history global multi-value matrix is now
COMPLETE — all 4 expectation-check dimensions on global diff-
history are multi-value tuple expressions: --kind (ADR-0217) +
--kind-not (ADR-0214) + --actor-id (ADR-0218) + --actor-id-not
(this milestone). The actor + kind family has 9 consecutive
single→multi widenings across all 3 retention surfaces. Per-
side single-value variants remain (ADR-0215 + ADR-0216
family); per-side multi-value tuples are the next natural batch
of milestones. Natural follow-up milestones — per-side multi-
value variants on diff-history (ADR-0215 + ADR-0217/0218/0219
family — 4 deferred Qs per side dimension), multi-value --kind
on retention history (ADR-0206 Q1), JSON envelope shape
documentation as a family-wide ADR.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.actor-id.multi
closes ADR-0203 Q2 by widening `--actor-id <uuid>` from
single-value to repeated flag (OR-semantic tuple expectation
check) on `retention diff-history` using the multiFlags
infrastructure from ADR-0183. Operator declares "I expect BOTH
events to have any of these N actors" and adapter throws on
mismatch with the side(s) named. Mirror of ADR-0217 (just-
shipped diff-history kind multi-value) on the actor dimension —
same one-shot break pattern as ADR-0199/0207/0210/0211/0214/
0217 (8 consecutive single→multi renames across the actor +
kind family). Restores within-action symmetry partially on the
actor dimension on diff-history — positive --actor-id is now
multi-value; negative --actor-id-not stays single-value
(documented as future Q for ADR-0205 Q1). Real cohort-positive-
expectation use cases on actor dimension — multi-operator state
transition verification (BOTH events authored by EITHER Alice
OR Bob canonical pair-of-trusted-operators forensic), on-call
cohort verification (BOTH events in on-call actor set of 5
SREs), system-account-pair verification (BOTH events from
EITHER migration SA OR backfill SA), CI gate with actor-tuple
positive (require both events match any of 3 expected service
accounts), compose with --kind multi-value (BOTH events
opt_out_set OR opt_out_cleared AND authored by Alice OR Bob),
compose with --actor-id-not single-value (BOTH events by Alice
OR Bob but NOT Carol cohort positive + single exclusion).
Breaking adapter rename DiffHistoryEntriesInput.actorId?:
string → actorIds?: ReadonlyArray<string>; session-recent code
from ADR-0203 with no external consumers contained scope
matches ADR-0199/0207/0210/0211/0214/0217 one-shot break
precedent. Adapter rewritten with Set-based O(1) membership
lookup; null actor_id never matches positive expectation by
construction (system events fail positive — operators wanting
"system OK" use --system-only instead); always-list error
format `actor_id in ['X']` for single-value + `actor_id in
['X', 'Y']` for multi-value (single-value error breaks from
ADR-0203's `actor_id 'X'` shape — acceptable since field
rename is itself breaking; consistent rendering across single
+ multi); <system> placeholder for null actor_id preserved.
CLI runRetentionDiffHistory reads via getMultiFlag("actor-id")
instead of getStringFlag; no per-occurrence validation needed
(UUIDs are free-form strings at CLI boundary; PG validates at
SQL layer matching ADR-0210/0211 history actor multi-value
pattern). JSON envelope renamed actorId: string | null →
actorIds: string[] | null matching ADR-0207/0210/0211/0214/
0217 array-or-null canonical multi-value envelope shape across
the family. Per-side --actor-id-a / --actor-id-b stay single-
value (documented as future Q for ADR-0215 family; deferred to
keep milestone scope focused). Asymmetry between global multi-
value and per-side single-value acceptable because per-side
semantically asserts THIS event must be exactly this actor
(singular by design — operator picking per-side already has a
specific actor in mind for each side). cli.ts helpText updated
from [--actor-id <uuid>] to [--actor-id <uuid> ...] indicating
repeatable + description extended explaining "repeatable +
BOTH events have any of the listed actors (OR-semantic tuple
expectation) + mismatch by either side on any actor not in the
listed set exits 1 + null actor_id never matches positive
expectation". Adapter test block rewritten + expanded from 7
to 11 tests under renamed "diffHistoryEntries --actor-id
expectation check (M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history
.actor-filter + .multi)" describe block — single-value accepts
both match, single-value throws A mismatch with new actor_id
in ['X'] format, single-value throws B mismatch, single-value
throws naming both sides when neither matches, single-value
renders <system> for null actor_id, multi-value accepts when
both have any of N expected actors (NEW), multi-value throws
when A doesn't match any of N with multi-value error format
(NEW), multi-value throws naming both when both events fail
the actor tuple (NEW), omits check when actorIds not set
backward compat, treats empty actorIds array as filter-not-set
(NEW), composes with eventKind check. CLI test block rewritten
+ expanded from 6 to 10 tests under renamed "runRetention
diff-history --actor-id (M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-
history.actor-filter + .multi)" describe block — threads
single-element array when --actor-id set once, threads multi-
element array when --actor-id repeated (NEW), omits when not
set backward compat, composes with --kind threading both
eventKinds and actorIds, composes with --actor-id-not
threading both as independent dimensions (NEW), adapter
mismatch error propagates exit 1 with multi-value error
format, JSON envelope echoes single-element array, JSON
envelope echoes multi-element array (NEW), JSON envelope
actorIds=null when not set, composes with per-side --actor-id-a
threading both global and per-side independently (NEW). Test
count: 9,076 → 9,083 (+7 net: adapter +4, CLI +4 but global
counter -1 from rewrite consolidation). ADR-0218 documents the
design + 10 rejected alternatives + 6 future Qs (per-side
multi-value --actor-id-a / --actor-id-b defer, --actor-id
@file.txt defer UUID lists scripted via xargs, multi-value
--actor-id-not closes ADR-0205 Q1 defer natural follow-up,
multi-value --actor-id on retention history N/A already
multi-value from ADR-0210, CLI-side dedup defer Set handles
fine, semantic-shape positive grouping --on-call-actors
shorthand defer operator-policy). The retention CLI's multi-
value flag coverage now spans the COMPLETE positive actor +
kind dimensions on diff-history — both --kind and --actor-id
are multi-value tuple expectations; only --actor-id-not (and

M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.kind.multi
closes ADR-0198 Q2 by widening `--kind <event-kind>` from

on mismatch with the side(s) named. Restores within-action

use cases — workflow audit asserting both events are in opt-
out workflow kinds (assert BOTH events have any of opt_out_set
or opt_out_cleared), migration-pair verification (assert BOTH
events are deletion or rebuild kinds), CI gate with kind-tuple
positive (require both events match any of these 3 expected
kinds before treating diff as legitimate state transition),
cohort-aware forensic comparison composing with --system-only
from ADR-0212 for layered assertion. Breaking adapter rename

recent code from ADR-0198 with no external consumers contained
scope matches ADR-0199/0207/0210/0211/0214 one-shot break

rewritten with Set-based O(1) membership lookup; always-list

`event_kind in ['X', 'Y']` for multi-value (single-value error
breaks from ADR-0198's `event_kind 'X'` shape — acceptable
since field rename is itself breaking; consistent rendering
across single + multi reads better than separate single-
value-special-case vs multi-value-list-case formats). CLI
runRetentionDiffHistory reads via getMultiFlag("kind") instead
of getStringFlag; per-occurrence validation via
isOptOutHistoryEventKind loop matching ADR-0200/0214 pattern —
exits 2 on FIRST invalid value with that value named (operators
reading error know exactly which --kind occurrence failed).
JSON envelope renamed kind: string | null → kinds: string[] |
null matching ADR-0207/0210/0211/0214 array-or-null canonical
multi-value envelope shape across the family. Within-action
symmetry restored on global diff-history — both --kind and
--kind-not now multi-value; per-side --kind-a / --kind-b stay
single-value (documented as future Q for ADR-0215 family;
deferred to keep milestone scope focused on closing ADR-0198
Q2 on global flag specifically). Asymmetry between global
multi-value and per-side single-value acceptable because (1)

has a specific kind in mind for each side), (2) operator
wanting per-side tuple expectation can use global --kind multi-
value if both sides should match the same tuple, (3) per-side
multi-value is a natural follow-up future Q with the same one-
shot break pattern. cli.ts helpText updated from [--kind
<event-kind>] to [--kind <event-kind> ...] indicating
repeatable + description extended explaining "repeatable +

either side on any listed kind exits 1". Adapter test block
rewritten + expanded from 5 to 9 tests under renamed

.opt-out.cli.diff-history.kind-filter + .multi)" describe

(+8 net: adapter +4, CLI +4). ADR-0217 documents the design +

M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.per-side.system-only
closes ADR-0212 Q2 + ADR-0215 deferred per-side actor-presence
boolean-pair by adding 4 per-side actor-presence flags
(--system-only-a, --no-system-a, --system-only-b, --no-system-b)
to retention diff-history. This is the 5th and final per-side
future Q completing the per-side expectation-check matrix on the
diff-history surface. The 4-flag intra-side mutual-exclusivity
design space resolves cleanly by reusing the ActorPresenceFilter
discriminated string union from ADR-0209/0212 per side — same
type at adapter level, CLI handles 4 booleans + intra-side
mutual exclusivity check + translates to 2 union values one per
side. Real asymmetric use cases — migration-to-human handoff
verification (A is system migration, B is human adjustment),
human-to-automation handoff (A is manual opt-out, B is system
sweep), system sweep audit pair (both sides system), operator
forensic discipline (both sides human), compose with kind
expectations (A is human opt_out_set + B is system
policy_deleted), compose with actor expectations (A is Alice's
human + B is system NOT Bob), symmetric assertion via per-side
flags equivalent to global but explicit when composing.
DiffHistoryEntriesInput gains 2 new optional fields
actorPresenceA?: ActorPresenceFilter + actorPresenceB?:
ActorPresenceFilter (ADDITIVE matching ADR-0215 precedent —
per-side semantically distinct from global not just renames;
global symmetric assertion stays for operators wanting "both
events" semantic). Adapter gains 2 new conditional blocks
positioned immediately after the existing global actorPresence
block (end of actor dimension chain) — single-side check (not
"both events" loop) throws with explicit "event A" / "event B"
prefix using established asymmetric rendering (positive shows
actual UUID since operator needs to see WHO authored event;
negative renders <system> placeholder since operator knows
excluded value). Check ordering — global actorPresence fires
first then per-side A then per-side B matching ADR-0215
ordering pattern. CLI parses 4 new boolean flags via
getBooleanFlag + 2 intra-side mutual-exclusivity checks (both
A flags set exit 2; both B flags set exit 2; A's and B's flags
don't interact — operators can set --system-only-a +
--no-system-b for asymmetric "A is system, B is human"
assertion canonical use case) + translates to 2 actorPresenceA/
actorPresenceB union values + threads to adapter. JSON envelope
gains 4 new boolean fields (systemOnlyA, noSystemA, systemOnlyB,
noSystemB) matching CLI flag names verbatim using established
boolean-echo convention from ADR-0209/0212 — operators read
literal flag state; intra-side mutual exclusivity at CLI
guarantees both never true simultaneously. Composition with
global — per-side composes freely; when both set BOTH check
fire global first (matches ADR-0205/0215 contradictory-
combination ordering). --system-only + --system-only-a is
redundant but harmless; --system-only + --no-system-a is
contradictory at data layer (global fires first surfacing
"expected both events to be system-authored" error);
--system-only-a + --no-system-b is the canonical asymmetric
pattern; --system-only-a + --system-only-b is equivalent to
global but explicit. cli.ts helpText extended for retention
diff-history usage line with [--system-only-a | --no-system-a]
[--system-only-b | --no-system-b] notation + 4-line description
explaining per-side actor-presence + intra-side mutual
exclusivity + A's-and-B's-flags-don't-interact + per-side-
fires-after-global semantic. 10 new adapter tests in a new
"PostgresTraceRetention.diffHistoryEntries per-side
actorPresence expectation checks" describe block — --system-
only-a accepts/throws-with-actual-UUID, --no-system-a accepts/
throws-with-<system>-rendering, --system-only-b throws (B
independent of A), --no-system-b throws with <system>, canonical
asymmetric pattern --system-only-a + --no-system-b accepts when
A=null + B=UUID, composition global --system-only fires BEFORE
per-side --no-system-a surfacing global error first, omits per-
side actor-presence checks when fields not set backward compat,
per-side A fires BEFORE per-side B in check order. 13 new CLI
tests in a new "runRetention diff-history per-side --system-
only / --no-system" describe block — exit 2 when --system-only-a
+ --no-system-a both set with explicit error, exit 2 when
--system-only-b + --no-system-b both set, --system-only-a +
--no-system-b allowed (different sides asymmetric assertion),
threads actorPresenceA/B='system_only'/'no_system' for each
flag, omits per-side actorPresence fields when neither set
backward compat, composes with global --system-only + per-side
--no-system-a both threaded global fires first at adapter, JSON
envelope echoes systemOnlyA + noSystemA + systemOnlyB +
noSystemB booleans, JSON envelope all per-side actor-presence
booleans false when none set, adapter per-side error propagates
exit 1 with per-side error format, intra-side mutual exclusivity
check fires BEFORE PG adapter call (capture length 0). Test
count: 9,045 → 9,068 (+23: adapter +10, CLI +13). ADR-0216
documents the design + 8 rejected alternatives + 6 future Qs
(per-side --system-only-X + per-side --actor-id-X contradiction
detection defer, JSON envelope unification to discriminated
string per side defer would be breaking, apply per-side actor-
presence to retention diff-timeline N/A list-style not per-
event-pair, apply per-side to retention history N/A list-style,
short-circuit LEFT JOIN when actorPresenceA='system_only'
optimization pairs with ADR-0212 Q3 + ADR-0213 Q5 family defer,
combined per-side actor-presence + actor-id consistency check
operators can express via not setting --actor-id-a defer). The
retention diff-history surface now has the COMPLETE per-side
expectation-check matrix — 5 dimensions × 3 modes (global
symmetric + per-side A + per-side B) = 15 expectation-check
flag families plus --with-actor-names: event_kind positive
(--kind ADR-0198 + --kind-a ADR-0215 + --kind-b ADR-0215),
event_kind negative multi (--kind-not ADR-0214 + --kind-not-a
ADR-0215 + --kind-not-b ADR-0215), actor_id positive (--actor-
id ADR-0203 + --actor-id-a ADR-0215 + --actor-id-b ADR-0215),
actor_id negative (--actor-id-not ADR-0205 + --actor-id-not-a
ADR-0215 + --actor-id-not-b ADR-0215), system presence
(--system-only/--no-system ADR-0212 + --system-only-a/
--no-system-a ADR-0216 + --system-only-b/--no-system-b
ADR-0216). Operators have unprecedented control over per-event-
pair forensic assertions — symmetric AND asymmetric
expectations across kind + actor + system-presence dimensions
in a single command; the matrix is now fully symmetric every
dimension has both global symmetric and per-side asymmetric
variants. Natural follow-up milestones — multi-value tuple
expectations on actor-id positive/negative (ADR-0203 Q2 +
ADR-0205 Q1), multi-value tuple expectation on global --kind
positive (ADR-0198 Q2), per-side multi-value variants combining
ADR-0215 per-side pattern with future multi-value tuples. The
retention CLI now has 18 actions with the most comprehensive
multi-flag coverage in the codebase; the per-side asymmetric
expectation-check matrix on diff-history is COMPLETE for the 5
actor + kind dimensions.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.per-side
closes ADR-0198 Q1 + ADR-0203 Q1 + ADR-0205 Q2 + ADR-0208 Q2 by
adding 8 per-side asymmetric expectation-check flags to
`retention diff-history` covering 4 of the 5 per-side future Qs
in one milestone. The 5th future Q (ADR-0212 Q2 --system-only-a/
--system-only-b/--no-system-a/--no-system-b per-side boolean-
pair) deferred since the 4-flag intra-side mutual-exclusivity
design deserves its own treatment. The 8 new flags pair with
their existing global counterparts — --kind-a / --kind-b (closes
ADR-0198 Q1), --kind-not-a / --kind-not-b multi-value following
ADR-0214 (closes ADR-0208 Q2), --actor-id-a / --actor-id-b
(closes ADR-0203 Q1), --actor-id-not-a / --actor-id-not-b
(closes ADR-0205 Q2). Real asymmetric use cases — forensic
state-transition verification (assert A is deletion event + B
is rebuild event in correct order), per-side actor attribution
(assert A is Alice's mutation + B is Bob's), per-side exclusion
with different excluded actors per side (A not migration SA + B
not CI SA), per-side multi-value kind exclusion (A is opt_out
workflow not maintenance + B unconstrained), hybrid global +
per-side belt-and-suspenders (global symmetric assertion +
per-side narrows further), maximum-discipline forensic
assertion combining all per-side variants. ADDITIVE not breaking
rename — DiffHistoryEntriesInput gains 8 new optional fields
(eventKindA, eventKindB, eventKindsNotA, eventKindsNotB,
actorIdA, actorIdB, actorIdNotA, actorIdNotB) preserving the
existing 5 global fields; per-side flags are semantically
DIFFERENT operations from global flags (assert different things);
operators wanting symmetric "both events" assertion continue to
use global; operators wanting asymmetric per-side use new flags;
breaking rename would lose the symmetric semantic. Check
ordering — global before per-side within each pair (kind →
kindA → kindB → kindsNot → kindsNotA → kindsNotB → actorId →
actorIdA → actorIdB → actorIdNot → actorIdNotA → actorIdNotB →
actorPresence). Each per-side check fires AFTER its global
counterpart; operators combining global + per-side see global
error first if global fails (matches ADR-0205 contradictory-
combination ordering). Per-side error message format — positive
shows actual unexpected value `expected event A to have
event_kind 'X' but A is 'Y'` mirroring ADR-0203 rendering;
negative names the side without showing value `expected event A
to have actor_id NOT 'X' but A matches` mirroring ADR-0205;
<system> placeholder for null actor_id preserved across per-
side. CLI parsing — 8 new flag parses in runRetentionDiffHistory
(getStringFlag + isOptOutHistoryEventKind validation for
--kind-a/-b, getMultiFlag + per-occurrence validation loop for
--kind-not-a/-b matching ADR-0214 pattern, getStringFlag for
actor-id-a/-b/-not-a/-not-b). JSON envelope gains 8 new fields
matching CLI flag names verbatim (kindA, kindB, kindsNotA,
kindsNotB, actorIdA, actorIdB, actorIdNotA, actorIdNotB) —
string-or-null or array-or-null shapes following established
conventions; note envelope-level actorIdA (operator's CLI flag)
vs result.actorIdA (actual diff output) are at different JSON
nesting levels operators distinguish via path. Composition with
global flags — per-side composes freely; when both global +
per-side set BOTH checks fire global first; --kind opt_out_set
+ --kind-a opt_out_set is redundant but harmless; --kind
opt_out_set + --kind-a retention_set is contradictory at data
layer (global fires first surfacing global error); --kind-a
opt_out_set + --kind-b opt_out_cleared is canonical asymmetric
pattern. cli.ts helpText extended for retention diff-history
usage line with all 8 new flags grouped by family
(kind/kind-not/actor-id/actor-id-not) + concise descriptions of
per-side semantic + independence from global + error format. 17
new adapter tests in a new "PostgresTraceRetention
.diffHistoryEntries per-side expectation checks (M6.7.zz.tenant
.opt-out.cli.diff-history.per-side)" describe block — --kind-a
accepts/throws-with-actual-kind, --kind-b throws (B independent
of A), --kind-a + --kind-b both pass when respective matches,
--kind-not-a accepts/throws-with-multi-value-list-format,
--kind-not-b throws independently, --actor-id-a accepts/throws-
with-actual-actor, --actor-id-a throws-with-<system>-rendering
for null actor_id, --actor-id-b throws independently, --actor-
id-not-a accepts/throws, --actor-id-not-b throws independently,
composition all 4 per-side flags check independently when their
sides match, composition global --kind fires BEFORE per-side
--kind-a surfacing global error first, omits all per-side
checks when none set backward compat. 17 new CLI tests in a new
"runRetention diff-history per-side expectations" describe
block — --kind-a/-b invalid value exits 2 each with offending
value named, --kind-not-a/-b invalid value exits 2 on FIRST
invalid occurrence each, threads eventKindA/B + eventKindsNotA/B
+ actorIdA/B + actorIdNotA/B as appropriate when each flag set,
omits all per-side fields when no per-side flag set backward
compat, composes with global --kind + per-side --kind-a both
threaded, JSON envelope echoes kindA + kindB + kindsNotA +
kindsNotB + actorIdA + actorIdB + actorIdNotA + actorIdNotB
when set, JSON envelope all per-side fields null when none set,
adapter per-side error propagates exit 1 with per-side error
format. Test count: 9,011 → 9,045 (+34: adapter +17, CLI +17).
ADR-0215 documents the design + 10 rejected alternatives + 6
future Qs (--system-only-a/-b + --no-system-a/-b per-side
boolean-pair closes ADR-0212 Q2 design space needs careful CLI
ergonomics for intra-side mutual exclusivity, per-side multi-
value --actor-id-a repeatable for tuple expectation defer, per-
side --kind-a multi-value for tuple expectation defer, JSON
envelope field name distinction between operator-input
expectations and result diff values future rename, apply per-
side pattern to retention diff-timeline N/A since list-style
not per-event-pair, combined per-side + system actor presence
--system-only-a + --no-system-b for "A is system + B is human"
defer). The retention diff-history surface now has 13
expectation-check flag families forming a 4×3 matrix — 4
dimensions (event_kind positive, event_kind negative, actor_id
positive, actor_id negative) × 3 modes (global symmetric, per-
side A, per-side B) plus the actor-presence pair (--system-
only/--no-system global only); operators have unprecedented
control over per-event-pair forensic assertions with symmetric
+ asymmetric expectations across kind + actor + system-
presence dimensions in a single command. The retention CLI now
has 18 actions with the most comprehensive multi-flag coverage
in the codebase — operators can express virtually any state-
transition assertion in a single command. The natural follow-
up milestones — --system-only-a/--system-only-b/--no-system-a/
--no-system-b per-side boolean-pair (ADR-0212 Q2), multi-value
tuple expectations on per-side variants (ADR-0198 Q2 / ADR-0203
Q2 family).
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.kind-not.multi
closes ADR-0208 Q1 by widening `--kind-not <event-kind>` from
single-value to repeated flag (OR-semantic exclusion expectation
check) on `retention diff-history` using the multiFlags
infrastructure from ADR-0183. Operator declares "I expect
NEITHER event to have any of these N event_kinds" and adapter
throws on match with the side(s) named. Mirror of ADR-0210
(history actor-id-not multi-value), ADR-0207 (diff-timeline
actor-id-not multi-value), ADR-0200 (diff-timeline multi-kind)
— same flag-repetition pattern + same one-shot breaking rename
+ same per-occurrence validation against the 4-value enum
matching ADR-0200 pattern (exits 2 on FIRST invalid value with
that value named) + same empty-array-as-filter-not-set
convention. Real cohort-exclusion use cases — workflow audit
excluding maintenance kinds (assert NEITHER event is
policy_deleted OR retention_set), anti-automation pair
verification (assert NEITHER event is a system-automation kind
before treating diff as deliberate human-policy work), CI gate
with kind-list exclusion (fail build if either event matches
any of these 3 kinds), cohort-aware forensic comparison
composing with --system-only from ADR-0212 for layered
assertion. Breaking adapter rename DiffHistoryEntriesInput
.eventKindNot?: OptOutHistoryEventKind → eventKindsNot?:
ReadonlyArray<OptOutHistoryEventKind>; session-recent code
from ADR-0208 with no external consumers contained scope
matches ADR-0199/0207/0210/0211 one-shot break precedent (the
workspace has established the breaking-rename pattern across
the actor + kind family — 6 consecutive single→multi renames
make this milestone mechanical). Adapter rewritten with Set-
based O(1) membership lookup; always-list error format
`event_kind in ['X']` for single-value + `event_kind in
['X', 'Y']` for multi-value (single-value error breaks from
ADR-0208's `event_kind 'X'` shape — acceptable since field
rename is itself breaking; consistent rendering across single
+ multi reads better than separate single-value-special-case
vs multi-value-list-case formats). CLI runRetentionDiffHistory
reads via getMultiFlag("kind-not") instead of getStringFlag;
per-occurrence validation via isOptOutHistoryEventKind loop
matching ADR-0200 multi-kind on diff-timeline pattern — exits
2 on FIRST invalid value with that value named (operators
reading error know exactly which --kind-not occurrence
failed). JSON envelope renamed kindNot: string | null →
kindsNot: string[] | null matching ADR-0207/0210/0211 array-
or-null canonical multi-value envelope shape across the
family. Within-action asymmetry documented — positive --kind
stays single-value while negative --kind-not now multi-value;
documented as future Q ("widen --kind to multi-value tuple
expectation closes ADR-0198 Q2 + restores within-action
symmetry"); deferred to keep milestone scope focused on
closing ADR-0208 Q1 specifically. Asymmetry acceptable
because (1) positive + negative semantically distinct
dimensions — negative path benefits more from multi-value
since "exclude these N noise kinds" is more common than
"include exactly one of these N specific kinds" (operators
with positive multi-value would typically just use no filter
and post-filter), (2) diff-timeline has BOTH multi-value
patterns established, (3) the 4-value event_kind enum makes
multi-value positive less compelling than multi-value
negative since operators wanting "any of these 3 kinds" can
equivalently use "neither of the remaining 1 kind" via
--kind-not (negative is strict superset of positive's
expressiveness). cli.ts helpText updated from [--kind-not
<event-kind>] to [--kind-not <event-kind> ...] indicating
repeatable + description extended explaining "repeatable +
NEITHER event has any of the listed event_kinds + match by
either side on any listed kind exits 1". Adapter test block
rewritten + expanded from 8 to 12 tests under renamed
"diffHistoryEntries --kind-not exclusion check (M6.7.zz
.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.kind-not + .multi)" describe
block — single-value accepts neither matches, single-value
throws A matches with new event_kind in ['X'] format, single-
value throws B matches, single-value throws naming both
sides when neither matches, multi-value accepts when neither
has any of N excluded kinds (NEW), multi-value throws when A
matches one of N kinds with multi-value error format (NEW),
multi-value throws naming both when both events match
different kinds in exclusion list (NEW), omits check when
eventKindsNot not set backward compat, treats empty
eventKindsNot array as filter-not-set (NEW), composes with
--kind expectation check (both pass when distinct kinds),
contradictory --kind + --kind-not surfaces kind check first,
composes with --actor-id-not (both checks fire independently).
CLI test block rewritten + expanded from 7 to 10 tests under
renamed "runRetention diff-history --kind-not (M6.7.zz
.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.kind-not + .multi)" describe
block — exit 2 on invalid --kind-not value, exit 2 on FIRST
invalid occurrence when multiple flags supplied with bogus
value named (NEW), threads single-element array when --kind-
not set once, threads multi-element array when --kind-not
repeated (NEW), omits when not set backward compat, composes
with --kind both threaded independently (multi-value kind-
not), adapter exclusion error propagates exit 1 with multi-
value error format, JSON envelope echoes single-element array,
JSON envelope echoes multi-element array (NEW), JSON envelope
kindsNot=null when not set. Test count: 9,004 → 9,011 (+7
net: adapter +4, CLI +3). ADR-0214 documents the design + 10
rejected alternatives + 6 future Qs (widen --kind to multi-
value tuple expectation closes ADR-0198 Q2 + restores within-
action symmetry, --kind-not @file.txt defer since 4-value
enum is bounded, --kind-not filter on retention history pairs
with ADR-0206 Q4 defer, --kind-not filter on diff-timeline
across all 3 paths pairs with ADR-0207 Q4 + ADR-0200 future
Q defer, CLI-side dedup of duplicates defer Set handles fine,
semantic-shape exclusion grouping write-mutations
--exclude-write-mutations shorthand operator-policy defer).
The retention CLI's multi-value flag coverage continues to
expand — retention history actor + actor-not multi (ADR-
0210/0211), diff-timeline actor + actor-not + kind multi
(ADR-0199/0207/0194/0200), diff-history kind-not multi from
this milestone (the first multi-value expectation check on
diff-history); the symmetric positive --kind multi-value
tuple expectation is the natural follow-up. The retention
CLI now has 18 actions with comprehensive multi-flag coverage
spanning the actor + kind dimensions across all 3 surfaces
with surface-appropriate semantics.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-timeline.system-only
closes ADR-0207 Q3 + ADR-0193 deferred Q by adding `--system-
only` / `--no-system` boolean flag pair to `retention diff-
timeline` substrate-side WHERE filter across all 3 dispatch
paths (pair-wise + N-way via --add-tenant + cross-table via
--cross-table + --add-table). Completes the --system-only /
--no-system family on all 3 retention surfaces — retention
history (substrate-side filter from ADR-0209) + retention diff-
history (expectation check from ADR-0212) + retention diff-
timeline (substrate-side filter across all 3 dispatch paths
from this milestone). Use cases — cross-tenant cohort-vs-system
audit (across 5-tenant cohort show ONLY system-driven retention
sweeps), cross-table human-only forensic timeline (across all 4
prunable tables show NO system events during incident window),
pair-wise migration verification (verify migration ran as
system actor on both tenants), compose with --actor-id-not for
layered exclusion (human cohort excluding migration SA cohort),
compose with --actor-id positive cohort (redundant but valid
"filter to human X" idiom). Mutually exclusive at CLI boundary
both set returns exit 2 with `--system-only and --no-system are
mutually exclusive` error before any PG query. Composes with
every existing diff-timeline filter (--actor-id, --actor-id-not,
--kind, --since, --until, --limit, --after-id, --before-id,
--range, --with-actor-names, --add-tenant, --cross-table,
--add-table) without restriction. All 3 diff-timeline input
types (DiffHistoryTimelineInput + DiffHistoryTimelineNwayInput +
DiffHistoryTimelineCrossTableInput) gain optional
actorPresence?: ActorPresenceFilter field reusing the exported
type from ADR-0209. Discriminated string union over two
booleans matches ADR-0209 + ADR-0212 precedent — mutual
exclusivity enforced at type level, adapter consumers see clean
3-state choice, CLI translates 2 boolean flags into single
union value after mutual-exclusivity check. Each of the 3
adapter methods gains the same conditional WHERE block
positioned immediately after the existing actorIdsNot block
before the eventKinds block — system_only adds `h.actor_id IS
NULL`, no_system adds `h.actor_id IS NOT NULL`, no params
needed (IS NULL / IS NOT NULL are SQL constructs). Mechanical
3-path symmetry matches established ADR-0193/0194/0199/0200/
0202/0207 pattern — every diff-timeline filter exists in 3
identical conditional WHERE clauses. CLI dispatcher
runRetentionDiffTimeline parses systemOnlyFlag + noSystemFlag
via getBooleanFlag ONCE at the top of the function before path
dispatch, mutual-exclusivity check, translate to actorPresence
single union value, thread to whichever of 3 adapter methods
is selected. JSON envelope gains systemOnly + noSystem boolean
fields on all 3 envelope shapes (pair-wise no-discriminator,
nway:true, crossTable:true) positioned after actorIdsNot
matching the actor-dimension grouping in the JSON shape +
ADR-0209 history boolean-echo convention. Composition with
--actor-id / --actor-id-not — --system-only + --actor-id is
contradictory at data layer (system has null actor_id; actor-
IN requires non-null UUIDs in IN list; both clauses fire at
SQL layer; PG returns empty result natural outcome), --system-
only + --actor-id-not is redundant but valid (system events
satisfy IS NULL OR != $N clause from ADR-0207), --no-system +
--actor-id is canonical "filter to human X" idiom (actor_id =
a implies non-null harmless), --no-system + --actor-id-not is
canonical "human cohort excluding X" idiom (distinguishes from
--actor-id-not alone which includes system events). Why
substrate-side not jq-side — LIMIT correctness + cursor-
pagination correctness + index usage same reasoning as
ADR-0193/0194/0207 for prior diff-timeline filters. Why
parallel implementation across 3 paths — matches established
ADR-0193/0194/0199/0200/0202/0207 pattern; same SQL clause
structure across 3 adapter methods + one CLI flag parse + JSON
envelope echo across 3 envelope shapes; mechanical and
reviewable. 9 new adapter tests in a new "PostgresTraceRetention
diff-timeline actorPresence filter" describe block: pair-wise
adds IS NULL when system_only, pair-wise adds IS NOT NULL when
no_system, pair-wise omits clause when not set, N-way adds
IS NULL positioned after tenant IN list, N-way adds IS NOT
NULL when no_system, cross-table adds IS NULL positioned after
table IN list, cross-table adds IS NOT NULL when no_system,
pair-wise composes with actorIds + actorIdsNot + actorPresence
+ eventKinds + joinActor (all 5 clauses present + LEFT JOIN +
correct param positions), pair-wise adds no params for IS NULL
/ IS NOT NULL clauses (LIMIT param position correct). 10 new
CLI tests in a new "runRetention diff-timeline --system-only /
--no-system" describe block: exit 2 when both flags set with
explicit error message, pair-wise threads actorPresence=
'system_only' when --system-only set, pair-wise threads
actorPresence='no_system' when --no-system set, pair-wise
omits actorPresence when neither flag set backward compat,
N-way threads actorPresence alongside --add-tenant, cross-table
threads actorPresence alongside --cross-table, pair-wise
composes with --actor-id-not + --no-system both threaded
independently, pair-wise JSON envelope echoes systemOnly +
noSystem when --system-only set, N-way JSON envelope echoes
both booleans when --no-system set, cross-table JSON envelope
echoes both booleans when --system-only set. Test count: 8,985
→ 9,004 (+19: adapter +9, CLI +10). cli.ts helpText extended
on BOTH diff-timeline usage lines (pair-wise + cross-table)
with [--system-only | --no-system] notation + descriptions
updated explaining IS NULL / IS NOT NULL filter semantic +
mutual exclusivity. ADR-0213 documents the design + 10 rejected
alternatives + 6 future Qs (partial PG index on actor_id IS
NULL defer until measured, --actor-presence flag on retention
diff cross-tenant point-in-time different surface defer, per-
side --system-only-a/--system-only-b on pair-wise diff-timeline
pairs with ADR-0203 Q1 + ADR-0205 Q2 + ADR-0212 Q2 family,
actorPresence filter on retention diff current-state comparison
defer different semantic, short-circuit LEFT JOIN when
actorPresence='system_only' since system events have no user
row optimization defer, JSON envelope unification across
--system-only family surfaces tagged-union shape). The
--system-only / --no-system family is now COMPLETE across all
3 retention surfaces — retention history (substrate-side
filter single path ADR-0209) + retention diff-history
(expectation check 2 fixed IDs ADR-0212) + retention diff-
timeline (substrate-side filter 3 dispatch paths from this
milestone). Operators get system-vs-human ergonomics on every
retention CLI surface with surface-appropriate semantics
(filter on list-style queries; expectation check on cross-
event diff). The retention CLI now has 18 actions with
comprehensive multi-flag coverage — 4 actor-related filter
families (--actor-id, --actor-id-not, --system-only,
--no-system) on list-style surfaces all multi-value or
boolean as appropriate; 5 expectation-check families on
retention diff-history (--kind, --kind-not, --actor-id,
--actor-id-not, --system-only/--no-system) plus
--with-actor-names.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.system-only
closes ADR-0205 Q3 by adding `--system-only` / `--no-system`
boolean flag pair to `retention diff-history` as expectation
check (not filter, since diff-history takes exactly 2 fixed IDs
not a list query). `--system-only` asserts BOTH events have
actor_id IS NULL (both system-authored); `--no-system` asserts
NEITHER event has actor_id IS NULL (both have UUID actor —
human or service-account). Mutually exclusive at CLI boundary
(both set returns exit 2 with `--system-only and --no-system
are mutually exclusive` error before any PG query matching
ADR-0209's history pattern). Composes with all existing
expectation flags (--kind, --kind-not, --actor-id,
--actor-id-not, --with-actor-names) without restriction.
Reuses the ActorPresenceFilter discriminated union from
ADR-0209 (same type, same CLI mutual-exclusivity, same JSON
envelope echo shape). DiffHistoryEntriesInput gains optional
actorPresence?: ActorPresenceFilter field. New expectation
check block positioned after the existing actorIdNot check
(actor-dimension group): system_only throws naming offending
side(s) with their actual actor_id value ("but A is '<uuid>'")
mirroring ADR-0203 positive --actor-id rendering — operators
need to see WHO authored the event if it wasn't supposed to
have a human actor; no_system throws naming offending side(s)
as <system> placeholder ("but A is <system>" / "both A and B
are <system>") mirroring ADR-0205 negative --actor-id-not
rendering — operators already know which side they expected to
be human, naming the sentinel suffices. Different rendering
reflects different information needs (positive needs to show
unexpected UUID; negative just names the side since <system>
is the excluded value). Check ordering: eventKind → eventKindNot
→ actorId → actorIdNot → actorPresence (kind-dimension first,
actor-dimension second with specific-actor checks before
presence checks). Real expectation-check use cases — system-
only audit pair verification (assert both events are scheduled
sweep events), human-only forensic comparison (assert neither
event is system before drawing responsibility conclusions),
compliance attestation gating (--no-system + --kind opt_out_set
asserts both opt_out_set + neither is system), migration
verification (--system-only + --kind retention_set asserts
both events are migration script signature). CLI threads
systemOnlyFlag + noSystemFlag via getBooleanFlag, mutual-
exclusivity check, translate to actorPresence single value,
thread to adapter, echo systemOnly + noSystem booleans in JSON
envelope (matching ADR-0209 history boolean-echo convention).
Composition with --actor-id / --actor-id-not — --system-only
+ --actor-id is contradictory at data layer (system has null
actor_id; --actor-id requires UUID; first check fires
surfacing actor-mismatch error — natural first-fail ordering),
--system-only + --actor-id-not is redundant but valid (system
events are NEITHER any specific UUID; both pass), --no-system
+ --actor-id is canonical "both must be human actor X" idiom,
--no-system + --actor-id-not is canonical "both must be human,
excluding X" idiom. Why expectation check not filter — diff-
history takes exactly two IDs nothing to filter; PG-side WHERE
filter would silently return zero rows on mismatch
indistinguishable from "IDs don't exist"; adapter throw with
clear message naming offending side(s) is loud and clear
matches ADR-0198/0203/0205/0208 precedent. Mismatch exits 1
(runtime path); CLI misuse mutual-exclusivity exits 2.
10 new adapter tests in a new "PostgresTraceRetention
.diffHistoryEntries actorPresence expectation check" describe
block: system_only accepts when both null actor_id, throws
naming A when A has UUID (with actual UUID in error), throws
naming B when B has UUID, throws naming both sides when
neither is system, no_system accepts when both have UUID
actor, throws when A is <system> (explicit <system> rendering),
throws when B is <system>, throws naming both when both are
<system>, omits check when actorPresence not set backward
compat, composes with --kind expectation check (both pass
when actor + kind expectations met). 9 new CLI tests in a
new "runRetention diff-history --system-only / --no-system"
describe block: exit 2 when both flags set with explicit
error, threads actorPresence='system_only' when --system-only
set, threads actorPresence='no_system' when --no-system set,
omits actorPresence when neither flag set backward compat,
composes with --actor-id-not + --no-system (both threaded
independently), adapter expectation error propagates as exit
1 with explicit error including actual UUID, JSON envelope
echoes systemOnly=true + noSystem=false when --system-only
set, JSON envelope echoes noSystem=true + systemOnly=false
when --no-system set, JSON envelope both false when neither
set. Test count: 8,966 → 8,985 (+19: adapter +10, CLI +9).
cli.ts helpText extended for retention diff-history usage
line with [--system-only | --no-system] notation indicating
mutual exclusivity + 4-line description added explaining
expectation-check semantic + mutual exclusivity. ADR-0212
documents the design + 10 rejected alternatives + 6 future
Qs (--system-only/--no-system on diff-timeline closes
ADR-0207 Q3 + ADR-0193 future Q, per-side asymmetric
expectation --system-only-a/--system-only-b pairs with
ADR-0203 Q1 + ADR-0205 Q2 family, short-circuit LEFT JOIN
when actorPresence='system_only' since system events have
no user row optimization, JSON envelope unification across
expectation-check flags tagged-union shape, apply
expectation-check semantic to retention prune --system-only,
--mixed-actors flag for "exactly one event is system other
is human" asymmetric uncommon defer). The retention diff-
history surface now has FIVE expectation-check flag families
forming 3×2 + 1 matrix — kind dimension (--kind + --kind-not)
+ specific actor_id dimension (--actor-id + --actor-id-not)
+ system actor presence dimension (--system-only +
--no-system from this milestone) + actor display
(--with-actor-names) — operators get full positive +
negative expectation gates across kind + specific-actor +
system-presence dimensions with consistent error-message
shape and uniform exit-code semantics. The --system-only /
--no-system family is now 2-of-3 surfaces shipped: retention
history (substrate-side filter from ADR-0209); retention
diff-history (expectation check from this milestone);
retention diff-timeline (substrate-side filter across all
3 paths from ADR-0207 Q3 + ADR-0193 future Q deferred). The
retention CLI now has 18 actions with --system-only /
--no-system support on both retention history (list-style
filter) and retention diff-history (cross-event expectation
check) — operators get system-vs-human ergonomics on the
two complementary audit-log surfaces with surface-
appropriate semantics.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.history.actor-filter.multi
closes ADR-0186 Q1 by widening `--actor-id <uuid>` from single-
value to repeated flag (OR-semantic IN positive filter) on
`retention history` using the multiFlags infrastructure from
ADR-0183. Restores the within-surface symmetry that ADR-0210
explicitly documented as a future Q — both positive --actor-id
and negative --actor-id-not are now multi-value on retention
history matching diff-timeline's symmetric design (ADR-0199
positive multi + ADR-0207 negative multi). Real cohort-inclusion
use cases — multi-actor cohort audit (auditor + reviewer +
approver triad), multi-admin attestation ("show every mutation
made by ANY of these 4 platform admins"), multi-SA observability
(monitor 3 migration SAs simultaneously), composed positive
cohort + exclusion ("any of these N expected actors EXCEPT
these M noise actors"). Breaking adapter rename
ListOptOutHistoryInput.actorId?: string → actorIds?:
ReadonlyArray<string>; session-recent code from ADR-0186, no
external consumers, contained scope matches ADR-0199 + ADR-0210
one-shot break precedent. SQL changes from single-value
h.actor_id = $N to multi-value h.actor_id IN ($N1, $N2, ...);
single-element IN ($N) is PG-equivalent to = $N so no observable
behavior change for single-value callers (same query plan).
Empty array treated as filter-not-set matching ADR-0199/0207/
0210 convention. No IS NULL OR prefix on positive filter unlike
negative — operators wanting "match these actors" do NOT mean
"and also include all system events"; PG IN returns NULL for
null actor_id which is correctly filtered out; operators wanting
mixed cohort + system compose with --no-system / --system-only
from ADR-0209 explicitly (mixed mode deferred as future Q). Why
duplicates not deduplicated at adapter — operators passing
--actor-id <a> --actor-id <a> get duplicate placeholders
verbatim; PG handles fine via IN semantic; substrate doesn't
dedup matches ADR-0207/0210 stance. CLI runRetentionHistory
reads via getMultiFlag("actor-id") instead of getStringFlag;
threads actorIds to adapter; JSON envelope renamed actorId:
string | null → actorIds: string[] | null matching ADR-0210
array-or-null canonical shape across the family. Within-surface
symmetry restored — before this milestone retention history had
asymmetric single-positive + multi-negative (positive --actor-id
stayed single-value from ADR-0186 while ADR-0210 widened
negative --actor-id-not to multi-value); after this milestone
both are multi-value matching diff-timeline's symmetric design.
cli.ts helpText updated from [--actor-id <uuid>] to [--actor-id
<uuid> ...] indicating repeatable + description extended
explaining repeatable + OR-semantic IN. Adapter test block
rewritten + expanded from 7 to 11 tests under renamed "actorIds
filter (M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.history.actor-filter +
actor-filter.multi)" describe block — single-element IN ($1),
multi-element IN ($1, $2), omits clause when not set, empty
array filter-not-set (NEW), composes with tenantId (multi-actor
+ tenant filter), composes with joinActor (LEFT JOIN + IN both
present), composes with actorIdsNot positive + negative both
threaded (NEW), composes with all filter dimensions (full param
array verified), returns rows matching the actors, returns empty
array when no rows match, duplicate actorIds values produce
duplicate placeholders (NEW) PG dedupes via IN semantic. Plus
updates to two cross-reference tests in the before-id + range
describe blocks (actorId: ACTOR_A → actorIds: [ACTOR_A] in
composition tests) and one cross-reference in actor-not block
(positive+negative composition test). CLI test block rewritten
+ expanded from 7 to 9 tests under renamed "runRetention history
--actor-id (M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.history.actor-filter +
actor-filter.multi)" describe block — threads single-element
array when set once, threads multi-element array when --actor-id
repeated (NEW), omits when not set backward compat, composes
with --tenant + multi --actor-id + --kind, composes with
--with-actor-names (multi actor + LEFT JOIN), JSON envelope
echoes single-element array, JSON envelope echoes multi-element
array (NEW), JSON envelope actorIds=null when not set, human-
format empty-result message preserved. Test count: 8,960 →
8,966 (+6 net: adapter +4, CLI +2). ADR-0211 documents the
design + 8 rejected alternatives + 8 future Qs (--actor-id
@file.txt for very large cohorts, CLI-side UUID validation per
occurrence, substrate-side dedup of duplicates, composite index
on (actor_id, occurred_at) for scale, --actor-name via meta.users
JOIN, PG IN-list chunking for 1K+ actors, mixed cohort + system
mode requiring (actor_id IN OR IS NULL), apply multi-value
widening to retention history's --kind defer since 4-value enum
makes multi less compelling). The actor filter family on
retention history is now symmetric multi-value matching diff-
timeline; diff-history stays single-value because per-event-pair
expectation check has different semantic (multi-value tuple
expectation deferred to ADR-0205 Q1 / ADR-0203 Q2). The retention
CLI now has 18 actions with multi-value --actor-id +
--actor-id-not support on the two list-style audit-log filter
surfaces (retention history + retention diff-timeline); operators
get cohort-inclusion + cohort-exclusion ergonomics with surface-
appropriate semantics under one consistent flag-name family.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.history.actor-not.multi
closes ADR-0206 Q1 by widening `--actor-id-not <uuid>` from
single-value to repeated flag (OR-semantic NOT IN exclusion) on
`retention history` using the multiFlags infrastructure from
ADR-0183. Mirror of ADR-0207's multi-value pattern on diff-
timeline; closes the within-family asymmetry where retention
history's --actor-id-not stayed single-value (ADR-0206) while
diff-timeline's went multi-value (ADR-0207). Real cohort-exclusion
use cases — service-account allowlist audit (exclude N known SAs
in one command), self-exclude reviewer cohort during incidents
(exclude my actor + on-call + SRE), compliance non-automation
filter (regulatory human review excluding 3-5 automation SAs),
cohort negative attestation (every mutation NOT made by platform-
admin cohort). Breaking adapter rename ListOptOutHistoryInput
.actorIdNot?: string → actorIdsNot?: ReadonlyArray<string>;
session-recent code from ADR-0206 with no external consumers
contained scope (matches ADR-0199 + ADR-0207 one-shot break
precedent — permanent two-field surface invites operator confusion
"which one wins when both set"; one-shot clean break beats
permanent two-field surface). SQL changes from single-value
`(h.actor_id IS NULL OR h.actor_id != $N)` to multi-value
`(h.actor_id IS NULL OR h.actor_id NOT IN ($N1, $N2, ...))`;
single-element NOT IN ($N) is PG-equivalent to = $N so no
observable behavior change for single-value callers (same query
plan). Empty array treated as filter-not-set matching ADR-0199/
0207 convention — operators may legitimately compute the list at
call site producing empty array (allowlist is empty); operator-
friendly. Why explicit IS NULL OR prefix preserved — PG NOT IN
returns NULL when actor_id IS NULL silently filtering system
events OUT (wrong intent — --actor-id-not means "exclude these
actors" which includes system events); explicit IS NULL OR prefix
restores correct inclusion same trap ADR-0206 documented now
extended to multi-value form. Why duplicates not deduplicated at
adapter — operators passing --actor-id-not <a> --actor-id-not <a>
get duplicate placeholders verbatim; PG handles fine via NOT IN
semantic substrate doesn't dedup matches ADR-0207 stance. CLI
runRetentionHistory reads via getMultiFlag("actor-id-not") instead
of getStringFlag; threads actorIdsNot to adapter; JSON envelope
renamed actorIdNot: string | null → actorIdsNot: string[] | null
matches ADR-0207's array-or-null canonical multi-value envelope
shape across the family. Within-surface asymmetry documented —
retention history's positive --actor-id stays single-value while
negative --actor-id-not now multi-value; documented as future Q
("widen --actor-id to multi-value on retention history would
close ADR-0186 future Q + restore within-surface symmetry");
deferred to keep milestone scope focused on closing ADR-0206 Q1
specifically. Asymmetry acceptable here because (1) positive +
negative semantically distinct dimensions — negative path benefits
more from multi-value since "exclude these N noise actors" is
more common than "include exactly these N specific actors", (2)
diff-timeline has BOTH multi-value (ADR-0199 + ADR-0207) for
operators wanting multi-value positive, (3) asymmetry operator-
visible via helpText + JSON envelope shape no silent behavior
change. cli.ts helpText updated from [--actor-id-not <uuid>] to
[--actor-id-not <uuid> ...] indicating repeatable + description
extended explaining repeatable + OR-semantic NOT IN + system-
events-included semantic. Adapter test block rewritten + expanded
from 8 to 11 tests under renamed "actorIdsNot filter (M6.7.zz
.tenant.opt-out.cli.history.actor-not + actor-not.multi)" describe
block — single-element NOT IN ($1) verified, multi-element NOT IN
($1, $2) with two placeholders verified, omits clause when not
set, empty array filter-not-set (NEW), includes system events
(IS NULL prefix preserved), composes with tenantId (multi-actor
+ tenant filter), composes with actorId (positive + negative
both threaded), composes with joinActor (LEFT JOIN + NOT IN both
present), composes with all filter dimensions (full param array
verified including multi-actor 7-param threading), returns rows
excluding listed actors (mock row + adapter result), duplicate
actorIdsNot values produce duplicate placeholders PG dedupes via
NOT IN semantic (NEW). Plus one cross-reference fix in system-
only describe block (`composes with actorIdNot + no_system` →
`composes with actorIdsNot + no_system`). CLI test block
rewritten + expanded from 6 to 8 tests under renamed
"runRetention history --actor-id-not (M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli
.history.actor-not + actor-not.multi)" describe block — threads
single-element array when set once, threads multi-element array
when --actor-id-not repeated (NEW), omits when not set backward
compat, composes with --actor-id positive + negative both
independently, composes with --tenant + --with-actor-names + multi
--actor-id-not, JSON envelope echoes single-element array,
JSON envelope echoes multi-element array (NEW), JSON envelope
actorIdsNot=null when not set. Test count: 8,955 → 8,960 (+5 net:
adapter +3, CLI +2). ADR-0210 documents the design + 10 rejected
alternatives + 7 future Qs (widen --actor-id to multi-value on
retention history closes ADR-0186 future Q + restores within-
surface symmetry, --actor-id-not @file.txt for very large lists,
CLI-side UUID validation per occurrence, substrate-side dedup of
duplicates, composite index on (actor_id, occurred_at) for scale,
--actor-name-not via meta.users JOIN, PG IN-list chunking for
1K+ excluded actors). The --actor-id-not family is now multi-
value uniformly across both filter surfaces — retention history
(this milestone) + retention diff-timeline (ADR-0207) — plus
single-value expectation check on the cross-event diff surface
(retention diff-history from ADR-0205); operators get cohort-
exclusion ergonomics with surface-appropriate semantics under
one consistent flag name. The retention CLI now has 18 actions
with multi-value --actor-id-not support on the two list-style
audit-log filter surfaces.
