M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.history.system-only
closes ADR-0186 + ADR-0206 + ADR-0205/0207 future Qs by adding
`--system-only` / `--no-system` boolean flag pair to `retention
history` for explicit null-actor filtering on the audit-log
surface. `--system-only` filters to events where actor_id IS NULL
(substrate-authored maintenance — prune sweeps, scheduled jobs,
expiry auto-lifts); `--no-system` filters to events where actor_id
IS NOT NULL (human / service-account authored deliberate
mutations). Operators wanting these queries previously jq-filtered
post-fetch which broke LIMIT correctness + cursor-pagination
correctness + index-usage parity — substrate-side WHERE is the
only way to get all three right (same reasoning as ADR-0186 +
ADR-0206 for value-based actor filtering). Mutually exclusive at
CLI boundary — both set returns exit 2 before any PG query with
"--system-only and --no-system are mutually exclusive" error.
Composes with every existing filter (--tenant + --table + --kind
+ --actor-id + --actor-id-not + --since + --until + --limit +
--after-id + --before-id + --range + --with-actor-names) without
restriction. Adapter changes — new exported ActorPresenceFilter
discriminated string union ("system_only" | "no_system");
ListOptOutHistoryInput gains optional actorPresence?:
ActorPresenceFilter field. Discriminated string union over two
booleans because (1) mutual exclusivity enforced at the type
level — impossible to express "both at once" in adapter input,
(2) adapter consumers see clean 3-state choice, (3) CLI translates
the two boolean flags into the single union value after the
mutual-exclusivity check. SQL adds one of two conditional WHERE
clauses (no parameters needed — IS NULL / IS NOT NULL are SQL
constructs not value comparisons): system_only adds h.actor_id
IS NULL; no_system adds h.actor_id IS NOT NULL. Position —
immediately after actorIdNot filter, before --since / --until
time-range clauses (matches existing actor-dimension grouping in
WHERE-clause assembly). CLI threads systemOnlyFlag + noSystemFlag
via getBooleanFlag, mutual-exclusivity check, translate to
actorPresence single value, thread to adapter, echo systemOnly +
noSystem booleans in JSON envelope (matching existing
withActorNames boolean-echo pattern from ADR-0185 — every CLI
flag has corresponding envelope field with same shape; mutual
exclusivity at CLI guarantees both are never true simultaneously).
Composition with existing actor filters — --system-only +
--actor-id is contradictory at data layer (system events have
actor_id IS NULL; --actor-id requires UUID; substrate returns
empty silently SQL natural outcome), --system-only + --actor-id-
not is redundant but valid (system events are NEITHER any specific
UUID by definition), --no-system + --actor-id is canonical "human
X's mutations" idiom (--no-system redundant since actor_id = $N
implies IS NOT NULL but harmless), --no-system + --actor-id-not
is canonical "human mutations excluding X" idiom (distinguished
from --actor-id-not alone which includes system events). Why
substrate-side not jq-side — LIMIT correctness, cursor-pagination
correctness, index usage parity (matches ADR-0186 + ADR-0206
reasoning). 8 new adapter tests in a new "PostgresTraceRetention
.listOptOutHistory actorPresence filter" describe block: adds
h.actor_id IS NULL when system_only, adds h.actor_id IS NOT NULL
when no_system, omits clause when not set, adds no params for
IS NULL/IS NOT NULL clauses (no placeholder pollution; LIMIT
param position correct), composes with tenantId + tableName
(system_only), composes with actorIdNot + no_system (redundant
but valid; both clauses present), composes with joinActor +
actorPresence (LEFT JOIN + IS NULL both present), returns rows
with null actor_id when system_only matches. 9 new CLI tests in
a new "runRetention history --system-only / --no-system" describe
block: exit 2 when both flags set with explicit error message,
threads actorPresence='system_only' when --system-only set,
threads actorPresence='no_system' when --no-system set, omits
actorPresence when neither set backward compat, composes with
--tenant + --system-only, composes with --actor-id-not +
--no-system (both threaded independently), JSON envelope echoes
systemOnly=true + noSystem=false when --system-only set, JSON
envelope echoes noSystem=true + systemOnly=false when --no-system
set, JSON envelope echoes both false when neither set. Test
count: 8,938 → 8,955 (+17: adapter +8, CLI +9). cli.ts helpText
extended for retention history usage line with [--system-only |
--no-system] notation indicating mutual exclusivity + 3-line
description added explaining IS NULL / IS NOT NULL semantic +
mutual exclusivity. ADR-0209 documents the design + 10 rejected
alternatives + 6 future Qs (--system-only/--no-system on diff-
history as expectation check pairing with ADR-0205 Q3,
--system-only/--no-system on diff-timeline across all 3 paths
pairing with ADR-0193 + ADR-0207 future Qs, partial index on
actor_id IS NULL for scale, short-circuit LEFT JOIN when
actorPresence='system_only' since system events have no user
row, per-side --system-only-a/--system-only-b on diff-history,
overloading --actor-id null sentinel rejected). The retention
history surface now has FIVE actor-related filter dimensions —
specific actor positive (--actor-id from ADR-0186) + specific
actor negative (--actor-id-not from ADR-0206) + system presence
positive (--system-only from this milestone) + system presence
negative (--no-system from this milestone) + actor display
(--with-actor-names from ADR-0185) — operators get first-class
system-vs-human + specific-actor inclusion/exclusion audit-log
workflows without LIMIT + pagination + index-usage correctness
problems of jq-filter workarounds. The `--system-only` /
`--no-system` family is now 1-of-3 surfaces: retention history
(substrate-side filter from this milestone); retention diff-
history (expectation check from ADR-0205 Q3 deferred); retention
diff-timeline (substrate-side filter across all 3 paths from
ADR-0207 Q3 + ADR-0193 future Q deferred). Subsequent milestones
close the remaining two surfaces mechanically following the
established per-surface precedent from --actor-id-not (ADR-0205
expectation check + ADR-0206 history filter + ADR-0207 diff-
timeline multi-value filter).
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.kind-not
closes ADR-0205 Q4 + ADR-0198 Q5 in spirit by adding `--kind-not
<event-kind>` exclusion expectation check on `retention diff-
history`. Inverse of ADR-0198's positive --kind expectation;
mirror of ADR-0205's --actor-id-not exclusion on the event-kind
dimension. Operator declares "I expect neither event to have
event_kind X" and adapter throws when reality differs. Completes
the positive/negative × kind/actor expectation matrix on the
diff-history surface: --kind (ADR-0198) + --kind-not (this
milestone) + --actor-id (ADR-0203) + --actor-id-not (ADR-0205) +
--with-actor-names (ADR-0204) — operators get full expectation
gates across both row-level dimensions with consistent error
shape (positive: "but A is 'Y'", negative: "but A matches").
Use cases — anti-deletion verification (assert NEITHER event is
policy_deleted before treating them as ordinary state
transitions), compliance gate excluding system-driven kinds
(human-policy audit assertion both are opt_out_* not retention_set
automation), CI gate on tier migration (assert this diff is
opt-out lifecycle not migration noise), forensic disambiguation
(this comparison is opt-out toggles not policy deletion).
Adapter changes — DiffHistoryEntriesInput gains optional
eventKindNot?: OptOutHistoryEventKind field; adapter exclusion
check fires after the eventKind expectation check (positioned
immediately after --kind so kind-dimension checks group together
before actor-dimension checks) with error message naming
offending side(s) compactly — single match yields "but A
matches", both match yields "but both A and B match"; asymmetric
rendering vs --kind positive check (which shows actual value)
because operator already knows the excluded value, naming the
side suffices, identical shape to ADR-0205 --actor-id-not.
CLI changes — runRetentionDiffHistory reads --kind-not via
getStringFlag, validates via isOptOutHistoryEventKind against
4-value OPT_OUT_HISTORY_EVENT_KINDS tuple from ADR-0170 (invalid
value exits 2 with explicit valid-values list mirroring ADR-0194
+ ADR-0198 patterns), passes eventKindNot to adapter, echoes
kindNot: OptOutHistoryEventKind | null in JSON envelope. NOT
mutually exclusive with --kind at CLI boundary — operators can
compose freely (both must be opt_out_set AND neither is
policy_deleted is valid + redundant; contradictory --kind X
--kind-not X surfaces the kind-check error first since order
matters). Mismatch exits 1 (runtime path matching ADR-0198 +
ADR-0203 + ADR-0205 + cross-tenant/cross-table mismatch
propagation). 8 new adapter tests in a new "PostgresTraceRetention
.diffHistoryEntries --kind-not exclusion check" describe block:
accepts when neither matches, throws when A matches with explicit
"A matches" error, throws when B matches with "B matches", throws
naming both when both match with "both A and B match", omits the
check when eventKindNot not set backward compat, composes with
--kind when distinct (both pass), contradictory --kind +
--kind-not surfaces kind check first, composes with --actor-id-
not both checks fire independently. 7 new CLI tests in a new
"runRetention diff-history --kind-not" describe block: invalid
--kind-not exits 2 with valid-values list, threads eventKindNot
when --kind-not set, omits when NOT set backward compat,
composes with --kind both threaded independently, adapter
exclusion error propagates exit 1 with explicit error including
expected + offending side, JSON envelope echoes kindNot when set,
JSON kindNot=null when NOT set. cli.ts helpText extended for
retention diff-history usage line with [--kind-not <event-kind>]
flag (third line of the usage block reorganized to fit the flag
families) + 2-line description explaining anti-kind verification
semantic + mirror-of-`--kind` framing. ADR-0208 documents the
design + 8 rejected alternatives + 6 future Qs (--kind-not
multi-value via comma-separated or repeated flag, per-side
--kind-not-a + --kind-not-b for asymmetric exclusion, --kind-not
filter on retention history pairs with ADR-0206 Q4, --kind-not
filter on diff-timeline pairs with ADR-0207 Q4, semantic-shape
exclusion grouping write-mutations, multiFlags-style multi-kind
exclusion). The retention diff-history surface now has THREE
expectation-check flag families forming a 2×2 + 1 matrix —
kind dimension (--kind positive + --kind-not negative from this
milestone) + actor dimension (--actor-id positive +
--actor-id-not negative) + actor display surfacing
(--with-actor-names) — operators get full positive + negative
expectation gates across both row-level dimensions with named-
actor audit readability uniformly on the cross-event policy diff
surface. The retention CLI now has 18 actions with --kind-not
support shipped on the diff-history surface; filter-side
variants (--kind-not on retention history + diff-timeline)
remain as ADR-0206 Q4 + ADR-0207 Q4 deferred future work
following the established `--actor-id-not` precedent.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-timeline.actor-not
closes ADR-0193 + ADR-0206 Q3 by adding `--actor-id-not <uuid>`
repeatable multi-value exclusion filter on `retention diff-
timeline` across all three dispatch paths (pair-wise + N-way
via --add-tenant + cross-table via --cross-table). Mirror of
ADR-0206's retention-history pattern on the diff-timeline
surface BUT multi-value to match ADR-0199's positive
multi-actor filter shape on diff-timeline — within-surface
consistency wins over cross-surface uniformity (retention
history single-value documented intentional divergence).
All three input types (DiffHistoryTimelineInput +
DiffHistoryTimelineNwayInput +
DiffHistoryTimelineCrossTableInput) gain actorIdsNot?:
ReadonlyArray<string>; SQL adds `(h.actor_id IS NULL OR
h.actor_id NOT IN ($N1, $N2, ...))` when set with non-
empty array — explicitly OR'd IS NULL so system events
INCLUDED (PG NOT IN returns NULL for null values silently
filtering systems out which is wrong intent); empty array
treated as filter-not-set matching ADR-0199 convention.
CLI uses getMultiFlag("actor-id-not") collecting repeated
flag occurrences, threads actorIdsNot to whichever of three
adapters dispatched, JSON envelope echoes actorIdsNot:
string[] | null across all three envelope shapes (pair-
wise no-discriminator, nway:true, crossTable:true)
positioned right after actorIds field for consistency.
NOT mutually exclusive with --actor-id — both clauses fire
independently at SQL layer; contradictory --actor-id X
--actor-id-not X returns empty silently (SQL natural
outcome). Why parallel implementation across three paths —
matches ADR-0193/0194/0199/0200/0202 pattern; mechanical
+ structurally identical addition mirrors how every other
diff-timeline filter exists three times. Use cases unblocked
— cohort drift detection excluding noise actors (5-tenant
cohort × exclude migration + sweep SAs), cross-table audit
excluding automation, self-exclude reviewer audit during
incident window, compose positive + negative for fine-
grained filter with overlapping actor cohorts. Rejected
alternatives — CLI-side jq filter (breaks LIMIT
correctness), single-value to match ADR-0206 retention
history (would diverge from diff-timeline's existing
multi-value actorIds; within-surface consistency wins),
NOT IN alone without IS NULL OR (silently filters system
events), IS DISTINCT FROM per-value (doesn't compose with
IN list), mutually exclusive with --actor-id at CLI
boundary (would block valid intentional overlap patterns
adapter natural empty result cleaner), substrate-side
overlap rejection (SQL handles correctly), pipe-separated
single flag (operators with shell pipes hit edge cases),
three separate flags per dispatch path (uniform flag
matches established pattern). Drawbacks — multi-value
asymmetry with ADR-0206 retention history (documented
intentional follows ADR-0186 vs ADR-0193/0199 divergence
future widening Q exists), includes system events by
design, contradictory composition returns empty silently
(acceptable obvious), no CLI-side UUID validation, three-
path parallel implementation (structural symmetry mirrors
established pattern), no pipe-separated single flag form
(repeated flag is canonical). 9 new adapter tests in a
new "PostgresTraceRetention diff-timeline --actor-id-not
filter" describe block — pair-wise single excluded actor
adds NOT IN ($N) WHERE clause verified, pair-wise multi-
excluded actors adds NOT IN ($N1, $N2) with multiple
placeholders, pair-wise omits clause when not set, pair-
wise treats empty array as filter-not-set, N-way single
excluded actor positioned after tenant IN list, N-way
multi-excluded actors, cross-table single excluded actor
positioned after table IN list, pair-wise composes with
actorIds (positive + negative both clauses present), pair-
wise composes with joinActor + eventKinds + actorIdsNot
(LEFT JOIN + NOT IN + IN all 3 present). 10 new CLI tests
in a new "runRetention diff-timeline --actor-id-not"
describe block — pair-wise threads single actorIdsNot
when --actor-id-not set, pair-wise threads multi
actorIdsNot when --actor-id-not repeated, pair-wise omits
when NOT set backward compat, N-way threads actorIdsNot
alongside --add-tenant, cross-table threads alongside
--cross-table, composes with --actor-id positive +
negative both threaded, pair-wise JSON envelope echoes
actorIdsNot when set, N-way JSON envelope echoes, cross-
table JSON envelope echoes, pair-wise JSON
actorIdsNot=null when NOT set. Test count: 8,904 → 8,923
(+19: adapter +9, CLI +10). cli.ts helpText extended on
both diff-timeline usage lines with [--actor-id-not
<uuid>] flag + 2-line description explaining excludes-
listed-actors + system-events-included + repeatable
semantic. ADR-0207 documents the design + 8 rejected
alternatives + 6 future Qs (--actor-id-not on diff-
history already covered as expectation check from ADR-
0205, widen retention history --actor-id-not to multi-
value pairs with ADR-0206 Q1, --system-only + --no-
system explicit null actor matching, --kind-not exclusion
companion symmetric flag on diff-timeline, composite
index on (actor_id, occurred_at) for scale, --actor-
name-not filter via meta.users JOIN). The retention CLI
now has 18 actions with --actor-id-not support spanning
THREE retention audit-log surfaces — retention history
(substrate-side filter from ADR-0206), retention diff-
history (expectation check from ADR-0205), retention
diff-timeline (substrate-side multi-value filter across
all 3 paths from this milestone) — operators get full
positive + negative actor expectations across all
audit-log surfaces with surface-appropriate semantics
(single-value vs multi-value) under one consistent flag
name.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.history.actor-not
closes ADR-0186 Q1 + ADR-0205 Q6 by adding `--actor-id-not
<uuid>` substrate-side WHERE NOT filter to `retention history`
— inverse of ADR-0186's positive --actor-id filter on the list-
style audit-log surface. Different semantic from ADR-0205's
exclusion expectation check on diff-history (which throws on
match in a 2-fixed-event compare) — this is a list-style query
filter excluding rows where actor_id matches. Use cases —
service-account-free human audit (--actor-id-not <ci-sa>
returns human + system events excluding SA), suspended-user
activity audit (everyone else's mutations during period),
self-exclude review (--actor-id-not $MY_ACTOR_ID for
operator's own activity exclusion), compliance non-automation
filter (regulatory "human review" event lists excluding
migration/sweep SAs). ListOptOutHistoryInput gains optional
actorIdNot?: string field; when set SQL adds `(h.actor_id IS
NULL OR h.actor_id != $N)` WHERE clause — explicitly OR'd IS
NULL so system events are INCLUDED (matches operator
expectation "everything not Alice" includes system events;
PG's actor_id != $N returns NULL for null values silently
filtering systems out which is wrong). Substrate-side not
jq-side filter for LIMIT correctness + future cursor-pagination
correctness + index usage parity (matches ADR-0186 reasoning).
Filter position right after positive actorId filter in WHERE-
clause assembly. CLI threads --actor-id-not via getStringFlag,
passes actorIdNot to adapter, echoes actorIdNot: string | null
in JSON envelope right after actorId field for consistency
with WHERE-clause ordering. NOT mutually exclusive with
--actor-id — both clauses fire independently; --actor-id alice
--actor-id-not bob returns Alice's events (Alice != Bob so
clause passes); contradictory --actor-id X --actor-id-not X
returns empty silently (no error operators see empty result
naturally — explicit error path adds complexity for unlikely
misuse). No CLI-side UUID validation matches established
pattern. Rejected alternatives — CLI-side jq filter (breaks
LIMIT correctness), use `actor_id != $N` alone (silently
filters system events losing operator intent), IS DISTINCT
FROM single-operator alternative (functionally equivalent
explicit form clearer for SQL readers), mutually exclusive
with --actor-id at CLI boundary (would block valid
composition), multi-value via repeated flag from day one
(defer single-value common case), inverse flag named
--exclude-actor-id (verbose --actor-id-not matches ADR-0205
naming), substrate-side rejection of contradictory
combinations (SQL empty result is natural outcome),
filter at result-mapping layer (same LIMIT correctness
problem as jq). Drawbacks — single-value exclusion only
(multi-value defer), includes system events by design
(operators wanting human-only need separate flag defer),
contradictory composition returns empty silently (acceptable
since contradiction is logically obvious), no CLI-side UUID
validation, no --actor-id-not on diff-timeline yet (different
surface defer), no --system-only / --no-system flag (operators
jq-filter defer). 8 new adapter tests in a new
"PostgresTraceRetention.listOptOutHistory actorIdNot filter"
describe block — adds (h.actor_id IS NULL OR h.actor_id != $N)
WHERE clause when set verified via SQL substring + params,
omits actorIdNot WHERE clause when not set, includes system
events (null actor_id) when filtering verified via SQL
substring, composes with tenantId (both filters present),
composes with actorId both threaded though contradictory at
SQL level, composes with joinActor + actorIdNot (LEFT JOIN +
WHERE clause both present), composes with all filter dimensions
(7-param threading verified), returns rows excluding the actor
when adapter returns filtered results. 6 new CLI tests in a
new "runRetention history --actor-id-not" describe block —
threads actorIdNot when set, omits when NOT set backward compat,
composes with --actor-id both threaded independently, composes
with --tenant + --with-actor-names + --actor-id-not (3-flag
combination), JSON envelope echoes actorIdNot when set, JSON
actorIdNot=null when NOT set. Test count: 8,890 → 8,904 (+14:
adapter +8, CLI +6). cli.ts helpText extended for retention
history usage line with [--actor-id-not <uuid>] flag + 2-line
description explaining excludes-that-actor + system-events-
included semantic. ADR-0206 documents the design + 8 rejected
alternatives + 6 future Qs (--actor-id-not multi-value via
repeated flag using multiFlags from ADR-0183, --system-only +
--no-system explicit null actor_id matching, --actor-id-not
filter on diff-timeline all three paths, --kind-not filter
companion symmetric flag, composite index on (actor_id,
occurred_at) for scale, --actor-name-not filter via
meta.users JOIN). The retention CLI now has 18 actions with
--actor-id-not support spanning TWO retention audit-log
surfaces — retention diff-history (expectation check from
ADR-0205) and retention history (substrate-side filter from
this milestone) — operators get full positive + negative
actor expectations across both cross-event and list-style
query surfaces with surface-appropriate semantics under one
consistent flag name.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.actor-not
closes ADR-0204 Q5 + ADR-0203 Q1 in spirit by adding
`--actor-id-not <uuid>` exclusion expectation check on
`retention diff-history` — inverse of ADR-0203's positive
`--actor-id` expectation. Operator declares "I expect neither
event to have actor_id X" and adapter throws when reality
differs. Use cases — tier-migration anti-actor verification
(verify production migration events were NOT authored by test
SA), suspended-user audit pre-check, CI gate excluding
automation, "this isn't me" forensic discipline.
DiffHistoryEntriesInput gains optional actorIdNot?: string;
adapter exclusion check fires after the actorId check with
error message naming offending side(s) compactly — single
match: "but A matches", both match: "but both A and B match";
asymmetric rendering vs --actor-id positive check (which shows
"A is '<actual>'") because operator already knows the excluded
value, naming the side suffices. Null actor_id (system event)
correctly passes when --actor-id-not is a UUID (null !== UUID).
CLI threads --actor-id-not via getStringFlag, passes actorIdNot
to adapter, echoes actorIdNot: string | null in JSON envelope.
NOT mutually exclusive with --actor-id at CLI boundary — operators
can compose freely ("both must be Alice AND neither is Bob" is
valid + redundant; contradictory --actor-id X --actor-id-not X
surfaces actorId-check error first since order matters but
both error messages are clear). Mismatch exits 1 (runtime path
matching ADR-0203 + ADR-0198 + cross-tenant + cross-table
mismatch propagation). No CLI-side UUID validation matches
ADR-0175/0186/0193/0203 deferred decision. Rejected alternatives
— --actor-id-not as filter limiting fieldDiffs (doesn't fit
diff-history takes 2 fixed IDs not list query), make match warn
not throw (silent gates lose safety), two flags --actor-id-not-a
+ --actor-id-not-b per-side (overkill defer), CLI-enforced
mutual exclusivity with --actor-id (would block valid
compositions adapter-level errors surface contradictions
naturally), render match as fieldDiff (actor_id isn't a field
in next_state JSONB throwing is loud), PG WHERE NOT silent zero
rows (indistinguishable from no-match throw clearer), inverse
flag named --exclude-actor-id (verbose --actor-id-not matches
established naming). Drawbacks — single-value exclusion only
(multi-value defer), no null-actor exclusion (operators jq-
filter for system-only audits), composition with --actor-id
can be contradictory (clear adapter error in either ordering),
no CLI-side UUID validation, asymmetric error rendering vs
--actor-id positive check (different rendering reflects
different info needs documented), no per-side --actor-id-not-a
+ --actor-id-not-b (defer pairs with ADR-0203 Q1). 8 new
adapter tests in a new "PostgresTraceRetention.diffHistoryEntries
--actor-id-not exclusion check" describe block — accepts when
neither event has excluded actor_id, accepts when both events
have null actor_id and excluded is UUID, throws when A matches
excluded with explicit error "A matches", throws when B matches
"B matches", throws naming both when both match "both A and B
match", omits check when actorIdNot not set backward compat,
composes with --actor-id when distinct (both pass), contradictory
--actor-id + --actor-id-not surfaces actorId check first. 6
new CLI tests in a new "runRetention diff-history --actor-id-not"
describe block — threads actorIdNot when set, omits when NOT
set backward compat, composes with --actor-id (both threaded
independently), adapter exclusion error propagates exit 1 with
explicit error, JSON envelope echoes actorIdNot when set, JSON
actorIdNot=null when NOT set. Test count: 8,876 → 8,890 (+14:
adapter +8, CLI +6). cli.ts helpText extended for retention
diff-history usage line with [--actor-id-not <uuid>] flag +
2-line description explaining anti-actor verification semantic.
ADR-0205 documents the design + 8 rejected alternatives + 6
future Qs (--actor-id-not multi-value tuple exclusion, --actor-
id-not-a + --actor-id-not-b per-side asymmetric exclusion,
--system-only + --no-system explicit null actor_id matching,
--kind-not exclusion companion symmetric flag, --actor-id-not
filter on diff-timeline different semantic, --actor-id-not
filter on retention history pairs with ADR-0186 existing
filter). The retention diff-history surface now has both
positive (--actor-id from ADR-0203) and negative (--actor-id-
not from this milestone) expectation checks composing with
event-kind expectation check (--kind from ADR-0198) + actor
display surfacing (--with-actor-names from ADR-0204) — operators
get full positive + negative expectation gates with named-actor
audit readability uniformly on the cross-event policy diff
surface.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.with-actor-names
closes ADR-0203 Q3 + ADR-0198 Q5 by adding `--with-actor-names`
boolean flag to `retention diff-history`, composing ADR-0185's
LEFT JOIN meta.users infrastructure with the diff-history surface.
Adapter `DiffHistoryEntriesInput` gains `joinActor?: boolean`;
when set the SELECT widens to include u.display_name + u.email
via LEFT JOIN on actor_id; result type
`DiffHistoryEntriesResult` gains required `actorIdA` +
`actorIdB` fields (always populated since ADR-0203 added the
actor_id column to the SELECT) and optional `actorDisplayNameA/B`
+ `actorEmailA/B` populated only when joinActor=true. SQL is
aliased h. consistently (with or without JOIN) matching ADR-0185
+ ADR-0190 pattern. CLI runRetentionDiffHistory reads
--with-actor-names via getBooleanFlag, threads joinActor to
adapter, passes opts.withActorNames into formatHistoryDiff which
appends `by <actor>` suffix on each event line using the
generalized formatActor helper from ADR-0190 (display_name
(uuid) → email (uuid) → raw uuid → <system> for null actor_id —
same rendering rules as ADR-0185/0190). Composes with --kind
(ADR-0198) + --actor-id (ADR-0203) without restriction. JSON
envelope gains withActorNames boolean discriminator + result
conditionally carries actorDisplayName/Email fields when set.
Minor breaking change in DiffHistoryEntriesResult — actorIdA +
actorIdB are now required where they were absent in ADR-0173;
data has always been available (actor_id column existed since
ADR-0170, SELECT widened in ADR-0203); making them required
reflects reality and gives operators always-on access for the
no-JOIN path; contained-scope no production consumer. Use cases
unblocked — one-command "who made each change" diff audit,
compliance review with named actor attribution, compose with
--actor-id expectation check + display for forensic discipline,
incident post-mortems with named actor timelines. Rejected
alternatives — always-on JOIN no flag (adds query cost defeats
opt-in pattern matches ADR-0185/0190), new action
diff-history-with-actors (adds CLI surface flag-on-existing
matches established pattern), INNER JOIN (drops orphan-FK +
null-actor events loses audit context), display_name only
without UUID (strips forensic disambiguation matches ADR-0185
stance), separate --actor-names-format flag (overkill formatActor
falls back naturally), conditional actorIdA/B only when JOIN set
(splits path between always-available data and conditional;
making required cleaner), required null display fields when
joinActor=false (changes JSON shape backward-compat broken),
cache user lookups across paginated calls (substrate stateless
operators wrap at their layer bounded JOIN cost makes
unnecessary at 2 rows per call). Drawbacks —
DiffHistoryEntriesResult minor breaking change (contained scope
new actorId* fields reflect always-available data), conditional
display fields on result (matches ADR-0185 OptOutHistoryEntry +
ADR-0190 TimelineEntry convention consumers branch via
result.actorDisplayNameA !== undefined), LEFT JOIN cost per
query when flag set (meta.users.id PK index-only + LEFT JOIN
doesn't expand rows + bounded by 2 rows negligible at typical
scale), cross-schema dependency on meta.users when joinActor
=true (substrate ships both tables together not regression), no
human-format change to field diffs (only metadata header gains
actor suffix; per-field actor attribution different surface
defer), no multi-tenant scoping on meta.users (same caveat as
ADR-0185/0190 cross-tenant actor renders with canonical
display_name matches operator intent "who did this?"). 8 new
adapter tests in trace-retention.test.ts in a new
"PostgresTraceRetention.diffHistoryEntries --with-actor-names"
describe block — omits LEFT JOIN when joinActor not set
backward compat verified, emits LEFT JOIN meta.users when
joinActor=true with display_name + email columns in SELECT,
returns actor info for both events when joinActor=true + users
exist (Alice + Bob both populated), returns null
actorDisplayName + actorEmail when actor has no user row
(orphan FK case for B only), returns null for system actor
(null actor_id for A), omits actor display fields when
joinActor is false (TypeScript undefined), always populates
actorIdA + actorIdB even without joinActor (from existing
actor_id column), composes with --actor-id expectation check
(joinActor + actorId both threaded). 9 new CLI tests in
retention.test.ts in a new "runRetention diff-history
--with-actor-names" describe block — threads joinActor=true to
adapter when --with-actor-names is set, omits joinActor when
flag NOT set backward compat, human-format renders 'by Alice
Smith (uuid)' suffix when names populated for each event,
renders <system> for null actor_id, falls back to email when
display_name is null, omits 'by ...' suffix when flag NOT set,
JSON envelope echoes withActorNames=true + actor fields when
set, JSON withActorNames=false when NOT set, composes with
--actor-id + --kind (all three flags threaded). Test count:
8,859 → 8,876 (+17: adapter +8, CLI +9). cli.ts helpText
extended for retention diff-history usage line with
[--with-actor-names] flag + 3-line description explaining LEFT
JOIN meta.users semantic + fallback rendering. ADR-0204
documents the design + 8 rejected alternatives + 6 future Qs
(--actor-name-equals filter, show user status alongside name,
display tenant_membership role would need additional JOIN,
per-field actor attribution different surface, --actor-id-not
exclusion to compose with display, apply --with-actor-names
pattern uniformly to other diff-* surfaces). The retention CLI
now has 18 actions with --with-actor-names support uniform
across THREE retention audit-log surfaces — retention history
(ADR-0185), retention diff-timeline (ADR-0190 across all three
paths), retention diff-history (this milestone) — operators get
human-readable actor attribution uniformly across single-tenant
audit-log + cross-tenant timeline diff + cross-event policy
diff workflows.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.actor-filter
closes ADR-0198 Q3 by threading `--actor-id <uuid>` through
`retention diff-history` as an expectation check (not a filter,
since diff-history takes exactly two IDs not a list query) —
mirror of ADR-0198's `--kind` pattern on the actor dimension.
Adapter SELECT widened to include `actor_id` column; result row
type gains `actor_id: string | null` field; new actorId
expectation check fires after the existing eventKind check —
validates `entryA.actor_id === input.actorId` AND
`entryB.actor_id === input.actorId`, throwing with explicit
'expected both events to have actor_id <X> but A is <Y> and B
is <Z>' on mismatch naming the offending side(s); null actor_id
renders as `<system>` matching ADR-0185 retention history
convention — operators reading mismatch messages can't confuse
the system-actor sentinel with a literal "null" string. CLI
threads `--actor-id <uuid>` flag via getStringFlag (no UUID
validation matching ADR-0175/0186/0193 deferred decision), passes
actorId to adapter call, echoes `actorId: string | null` in JSON
envelope matching ADR-0193 convention. Composes with `--kind`
from ADR-0198 — both expectations applied independently, either
mismatch throws. Why expectation-check not filter — diff-history
takes exactly two IDs not a list, flag semantic shifts from
filter to expectation check matching ADR-0198's pattern; PG-side
WHERE filter would silently return zero rows on mismatch
indistinguishable from "IDs don't exist", adapter throw with
clear message naming the offending side is loud and clear.
Mismatch exits 1 (runtime path matching cross-tenant + cross-
table + eventKind mismatch error propagation), invalid input
exits 2 (CLI misuse). Use cases unblocked — compliance script
asserting actor match ('--actor-id <alice>' diff fails when
accidentally comparing across actors), forensic analysis gated
on actor (compose with --kind for fine-grained expectation
checks like '--actor-id <suspect> --kind opt_out_set'), CI gate
verifying service-account migration ('--actor-id <migration-sa>
--kind retention_set' exits 1 if migration ran as wrong actor),
detect inadvertent cross-actor comparison (operator forensic
discipline — fails loudly when picking wrong ID for either
side). Rejected alternatives — --actor-id as filter limiting
fieldDiffs (doesn't match per-event semantic of actor_id which
is row-level not field-level), make mismatch warn not throw
(silent gates lose safety property), two flags --actor-id-a +
--actor-id-b for per-side expectations (overkill for v1 defer),
PG-side WHERE clause validation (would silently return zero
rows operators can't distinguish from missing IDs), type adapter
input as string | null to allow null filtering (couples shape
to null-actor use case operators jq-filter), JSON envelope field
named expectedActorId (verbose; actorId matches ADR-0193
convention), render mismatch as fieldDiff (actor_id isn't field
in next_state JSONB throwing is loud), use actorId IN filter
semantic (would silently return zero rows expectation-check
throw clearer). Drawbacks — semantic shift from filter to
expectation check (mitigated by explicit error message matching
ADR-0198 caveat), no null-actor sentinel for input (operators
wrap with jq matches ADR-0186 null-actor stance), no CLI-side
UUID validation matches deferred decision, single-actor only no
multi-value tuple (different shape than multi-actor OR-filter
from ADR-0199 since expectation has "all must be one of N"
semantic vs filter's "any match"), no per-event expectation
(operators with different per-side expectations jq-filter JSON
defer), two new adapter errors with similar shape (same-tenant/
same-table/eventKind already throw with explicit error actorId
joins family acceptable structural consistency). 7 new adapter
tests in trace-retention.test.ts in a new "PostgresTraceRetention
.diffHistoryEntries --actor-id expectation check" describe block
— accepts when both events have expected actor_id, throws when
event A's actor mismatches with explicit error naming side A,
throws when event B's actor mismatches naming side B, throws
naming both sides when neither matches (including <system>
rendering for null actor_id), renders <system> for null actor_id
in mismatch message, omits the check when actorId not set
backward compat verified, composes with eventKind check (both
pass). 6 new CLI tests in retention.test.ts in a new
"runRetention diff-history --actor-id" describe block — threads
actorId to adapter when --actor-id set, omits actorId when
--actor-id NOT set backward compat, composes with --kind
threading both eventKind and actorId, adapter mismatch error
propagates exit 1 with explicit error including expected +
actual actors, JSON envelope echoes actorId field when set,
JSON envelope actorId=null when NOT set. Test count: 8,846 →
8,859 (+13: adapter +7, CLI +6). cli.ts helpText extended for
retention diff-history usage line with [--actor-id <uuid>] flag
+ 2-line description explaining expectation-check semantic +
mismatch-exits-1 contract + <system> rendering for null
actor_id. ADR-0203 documents the design + 8 rejected
alternatives + 6 future Qs (per-side --actor-id-a + --actor-id-b
for asymmetric expectations, --actor-id <a>|<b>|<c> multi-value
expectation, --with-actor-names on diff-history LEFT JOIN
meta.users for the two events' actors, --actor-name-equals
filter via meta.users lookup pairing with ADR-0186 Q3, CLI-side
UUID validation matching ADR-0175 deferred decision,
--system-only flag for both events being system-authored).
The retention CLI now has 18 actions with --actor-id support
uniform across THREE retention diff family members — retention
history (filter from ADR-0186), retention diff-timeline (filter
from ADR-0193/0199 across all three paths), retention diff-
history (expectation check from this milestone) — semantics
adapt to the surface (list-style filter vs cross-event
expectation check) but the flag name + UUID input + helpText
behavioral note are consistent across the family.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-timeline.range
closes ADR-0197 Q1 + ADR-0201 Q1 by mirroring ADR-0201's `--range
<after-id>..<before-id>` window-cursor flag onto all three
`retention diff-timeline` dispatch paths (pair-wise + N-way via
--add-tenant + cross-table via --cross-table). Pure CLI delivery
— no adapter changes since all three diff-timeline adapter inputs
already accept both afterId + beforeId simultaneously (substrate-
level range-cursor preserved across ADR-0195 + ADR-0197); --range
is the explicit operator gesture to opt into the range semantic
that the CLI was previously gating. Same parse + validation +
mutual-exclusivity pattern as ADR-0201 — git-like `..` separator,
exit 2 on missing separator + empty halves + > 2 parts, exit 2
when combined with bare --after-id or --before-id (since --range
sets both), bare-flag mutual-exclusivity error updated to suggest
--range. CLI dispatcher reads rangeFlag once + computes effective
afterId/beforeId from either rangeFlag halves or bare flags;
threaded to whichever of three adapters dispatched. JSON envelope
gains range: string | null field on all three envelope shapes
(pair-wise no-discriminator, nway:true, crossTable:true) echoing
operator's literal input + afterId + beforeId fields ALSO
populate when --range set for jq scripts reading either path.
Why parallel implementation across all three paths — the three
paths already use shared cursor + JSON envelope code; adding
range to each is mechanical and structurally identical (mirrors
how every other diff-timeline filter from --actor-id through
--kind through --after-id/--before-id already exists three
times); one milestone covers all three because the lift is
identical and the operator-facing surface stays consistent.
Window semantics depend on ASC ordering — diff-timeline orders
ASC (chronological) so --range <a>..<b> returns events newer
than a AND older than b (operators reversing cursor order get
empty results correctly); INVERSE direction from retention
history's DESC range semantic documented in helpText. Use cases
unblocked — anchor-based bidirectional cohort audit (5-tenant
cohort × incident-trigger..post-incident-cursor window), cross-
table window scoped to migration period (one tenant × 3 tables
× migration-start..migration-end), per-actor per-window forensic
narrowing composing multi-actor × multi-kind × range cursor in
one command, CI gate "no mutations in protected window" via jq
on result.entries length. Rejected alternatives — single-path
delivery (would force N-way + cross-table operators to bare
flags only; symmetric coverage matches established pattern),
different separator on diff-timeline vs history (confuses
operators learning both surfaces; same `..` is consistent),
reverse range semantic on diff-timeline (treat first half as
upper bound; would diverge from ADR-0201 convention where first
half is always --after-id cursor), two flags --from + --to
(verbose loses single-flag ergonomic), adapter-side accept
range parameter directly (pushes parsing into substrate where
it doesn't belong CLI is right boundary), no mutual-exclusivity
check (could mask operator confusion explicit error helps).
Drawbacks — parallel implementation across three paths
(structural symmetry mirrors established pattern), no adapter
changes pure CLI ergonomics, `..` separator strictly 2 dots
not 3 (matches ADR-0201), no CLI-side UUID validation matches
deferred decision, range envelope echoes raw operator input
matches ADR-0201, window semantics depend on ASC ordering vs
DESC on retention history (documented in helpText). 14 new CLI
tests in a new "runRetention diff-timeline --range" describe
block — pair-wise parses --range and threads both cursors, N-way
threads alongside --add-tenant, cross-table threads alongside
--cross-table (3 path coverage), exits 2 on missing separator
+ empty after-id half + empty before-id half + with --after-id
+ with --before-id (5 exit-2 cases), bare --after-id + --before-
id without --range error message points at --range, JSON
envelope echoes range + afterId + beforeId on pair-wise + JSON
range=null when not set, JSON envelope echoes range on N-way
path + cross-table path (3 envelope-shape tests), composes with
--actor-id + --kind + --with-actor-names + --since verified
multi-actor + multi-kind + window cursor + actor JOIN in one
command. Test count: 8,832 → 8,846 (+14). cli.ts helpText
extended on both diff-timeline usage lines with [--range <after-
id>..<before-id>] + descriptions explaining single-flag window-
cursor semantic + mutual exclusivity rules. ADR-0202 documents
the design + 6 rejected alternatives + 6 future Qs (--range on
diff-history likely not needed since diff-history takes 2
positional IDs not a list, strict-inclusive >=/<= cursor
semantic same as ADR-0195 Q6 + ADR-0201 Q2, range bounds
visualization in human output, --range @file.txt batch, parsed-
object envelope form, composite index for scale). The retention
CLI now has 18 actions with window-cursor pagination uniform
across BOTH audit-log surfaces — retention history --range from
ADR-0201 (DESC) and all three retention diff-timeline paths
--range from this milestone (ASC). Bidirectional pagination
(--after-id / --before-id) + window cursor (--range) +
multi-actor + multi-kind + cohort comparison (N-way + cross-
table) all compose across the diff-timeline surface in a single
command.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.history.range
closes ADR-0196 Q2 by adding `--range <after-id>..<before-id>`
single-flag window-cursor pagination to `retention history`. ADR-
0196 documented that ListOptOutHistoryInput accepts BOTH afterId
AND beforeId simultaneously at the adapter layer (substrate-level
range-cursor preserved for programmatic consumers) while the CLI
rejected both flags at boundary as "operators almost always meant
one of them"; --range is the explicit operator-side gesture
saying "I deliberately want both" leveraging the existing adapter
support. Pure CLI delivery — no adapter changes. Format `<after-
id>..<before-id>` uses git-like commit-range syntax (UUIDs don't
contain dots so no ambiguity; single-flag value is shell-quote-
friendly). Mutually exclusive with bare --after-id / --before-id
(combining is operator confusion since --range is already setting
both → exit 2 with clear error). Parse validation rejects missing
separator, empty halves, or more than 2 parts. No CLI-side UUID
validation matches deferred decision across cursor work. JSON
envelope gains range: string | null field echoing operator's
literal input; afterId + beforeId fields ALSO populate when
--range is set so jq scripts reading either path get correct
values; range:null when not set. Existing mutual-exclusivity
error for bare --after-id + --before-id (without --range) updated
to suggest --range as the proper window-cursor flag. Use cases
unblocked — investigation window between two known events
(--range <trigger>..<resolution>), tier-migration audit window,
two-pass forensic narrowing (page through wide window then zoom
with --range on suspect cursors), CI gate for audit-log emptiness
in a window. Rejected alternatives — two flags --from + --to
(verbose; loses single-flag ergonomic value), positional pair
syntax (unusual flag form), : separator (less mnemonic than git's
..), auto-relax bare flags mutual exclusivity (silently changes
ADR-0196 behavior), add to diff-timeline in same milestone
(scope creep matches ADR-0197 Q1 documentation), validate parts
as UUIDs at CLI (matches deferred-validation pattern), partial
range syntax (collapses with existing bare flags). Drawbacks —
single-surface delivery (only retention history this milestone;
diff-timeline --range deferred to future milestone), `..`
separator requires exactly 2 dots (3 dots `...` like git's other
syntax errors out), no CLI-side UUID validation, range envelope
echoes raw operator input (parsed object form deferred), no
partial-range support (already what bare flags do), window
semantics depend on DESC ordering (operators reversing cursor
order produce empty results correctly). 10 new CLI tests —
parses --range and threads both cursors to adapter, exits 2 on
missing separator + empty after half + empty before half + with
--after-id + with --before-id (5 exit-2 cases), JSON envelope
echoes range field + afterId + beforeId when set, JSON range=
null when not set, composes with all other filters (--tenant +
--table + --kind + --actor-id + --range + --limit), bare flags
mutually-exclusive message now suggests --range as the window-
cursor approach. cli.ts helpText extended for retention history
usage line with [--range <after-id>..<before-id>] + 5-line
description explaining the single-flag window-cursor semantic +
mutual exclusivity rules. ADR-0201 documents the design + 7
rejected alternatives + 6 future Qs (--range on diff-timeline
all three paths pairing with ADR-0197 Q1, --range strict-
inclusive >=/<= semantic same as ADR-0195 Q6, visualization of
range bounds in human output, --range @file.txt batch, range
envelope as parsed object {after, before}, partial range syntax
collapse). The retention CLI now has 18 actions with window-
cursor pagination on the audit-log surface — operators get
one-flag operator-friendly access to the substrate's range-
cursor support that ADR-0196 documented but deliberately gated
behind explicit operator gesture; bidirectional pagination
(--after-id/--before-id) + window cursor (--range) now compose
across the retention history filter dimensions (tenant + table
+ kind + actor + since + until + limit).
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-timeline.multi-kind
closes ADR-0194 Q1 by making `--kind <event-kind>` repeatable on
`retention diff-timeline` across all three dispatch paths via the
multiFlags infrastructure — mirror of ADR-0199's multi-actor
shape on the kind filter dimension. Single value scopes to one
event_kind; multiple values build OR-semantic IN clause ("every
opt_out_set OR opt_out_cleared event"). Zero values preserves
no-filter behavior. Breaking adapter type rename — all three
diff-timeline input types rename eventKind?: OptOutHistoryEventKind
→ eventKinds?: ReadonlyArray<OptOutHistoryEventKind>; contained
scope matches ADR-0199's one-shot break pattern. CLI uses
getMultiFlag("kind") + validates each occurrence individually
via isOptOutHistoryEventKind (exit 2 with the offending value
named on any invalid); single-element validates → IN ($N),
multi-element validates → IN ($N1, $N2, ...). Retention history
+ retention diff-history keep eventKind: single intentionally
— history is list-style with single-kind filter common,
diff-history is per-event-pair expectation check (different
semantic). JSON envelope renames kind: OptOutHistoryEventKind |
null → kinds: OptOutHistoryEventKind[] | null across all three
diff-timeline envelope shapes (pair-wise no-discriminator,
nway:true, crossTable:true). Use cases unblocked — opt-out
lifecycle audit (--kind opt_out_set --kind opt_out_cleared
across cohort), per-actor per-kinds forensics (Alice OR Bob ×
opt_out_set OR opt_out_cleared), positive exclusion of noise
kinds (3-of-4 kinds in one filter), compliance attestation
across cohort × kinds. Rejected alternatives — keep eventKind
+ add eventKinds alongside (two-field surface; matches ADR-0199
decision), --kind a|b|c pipe-separated (shell-quoting concern),
--kinds plural alias (adds CLI surface), validate batch all at
once (per-value validation gives crisper error), reject
duplicates at CLI (PG handles fine), PG enum-array
`= ANY($N::text[])` (inconsistent with substrate's positional-
placeholder pattern). Drawbacks — breaking type + envelope
rename (contained scope same one-shot pattern as ADR-0199),
retention history eventKind stays single divergence
(intentional documented), retention diff-history eventKind
stays single different semantic (expectation check ADR-0198
Q2 covers multi-kind), OR-semantic only no NOT filter (defer),
no duplicate dedup (PG handles). Adapter test block rewritten +
expanded from 7 to 11 tests covering single + multi-kind across
all three dispatch paths + empty-array edge case + composition
with actorIds. CLI test block rewritten + expanded from 9 to
12 tests covering invalid-on-any-occurrence exit 2 + single +
multi-kind threading + JSON envelope echoes on all three paths
+ composition with multi-actor. Test count: 8,815 → 8,822 (+7
net: adapter +4, CLI +3). cli.ts helpText extended for both
diff-timeline usage lines noting --actor-id + --kind both
repeatable with OR-semantic filter behavior. ADR-0200 documents
the design + 6 rejected alternatives + 5 future Qs (widen
retention history's eventKind to eventKinds for consistency,
--kind-not exclusion, multi-kind expectation on diff-history
pairing with ADR-0198 Q2, composite index on (event_kind,
occurred_at) for scale, --kind @file.txt overkill since only 4
valid values exist). The retention CLI now has 18 actions with
multi-value OR filters uniform across both actor + kind
dimensions on the diff-timeline surface — operators get cohort-
based forensics composing (N tenants OR M actors) × (P kinds)
× (time window) × (cursor pagination) × (cross-table or N-way
or pair-wise dispatch) in a single command across the three
diff-timeline paths.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-timeline.multi-actor
closes ADR-0193 Q1 by making `--actor-id <uuid>` repeatable on
`retention diff-timeline` across all three dispatch paths (pair-
wise + N-way via --add-tenant + cross-table via --cross-table)
via the multiFlags infrastructure from ADR-0183/0188. Single
value scopes to one actor's events; multiple values build an
OR-semantic IN-clause filter ("Alice OR Bob's mutations"). Zero
values (flag omitted) preserves no-actor-filter behavior. Breaking
adapter type change — all three diff-timeline input types rename
actorId?: string → actorIds?: ReadonlyArray<string>; contained
scope (session-recent code from ADR-0193, no external consumers,
no production surface affected); one-shot clean break beats
permanent two-field surface; same shape as ADR-0183's tenantIds
+ ADR-0188's tableNames consistent across N-way collection
inputs. SQL builds positional placeholders for IN clause: single-
element array yields `h.actor_id IN ($N)` equivalent to old
`h.actor_id = $N` (PG handles both identically), multi-element
yields `h.actor_id IN ($N1, $N2, ...)`. Empty array treated as
filter-not-set (no WHERE clause added). CLI uses getMultiFlag
("actor-id") returning string[]; threads to actorIds when
length > 0. JSON envelope renames actorId: string | null →
actorIds: string[] | null across all three envelope shapes
(pair-wise no-discriminator, nway:true, crossTable:true). The
retention history surface keeps actorId: string (single value)
intentionally — divergence with diff-timeline's actorIds
documented as future Q; separate milestone could widen if
measured demand emerges (operators currently chain two
commands for the rare multi-actor on history case). Use cases
unblocked — two-actor cohort forensics ("Alice OR Bob's
mutations across both tenants on workflow_traces during audit
window"), service-account allowlist audit (3 SAs + cross-cohort
+ --kind retention_set), per-team mutation audit on shared
table (3 team leads × cross-table), compliance reviewer audit
set (5 auditors × 5-tenant regulated cohort × full Q1 export).
Rejected alternatives — keep actorId + add actorIds alongside
(two-field surface complicated by merge/reject logic; one-shot
rename cleaner), actorId as string | ReadonlyArray<string> union
(forces every consumer to discriminate), CLI builds OR via jq
post-filter (breaks LIMIT correctness + cursor pagination same
trap ADR-0193 closed), PG `= ANY($N::uuid[])` instead of IN
positional placeholders (cleaner SQL but inconsistent with
substrate's positional-placeholder pattern throughout), widen
retention history actorId in same milestone (scope creep defer),
--actor-id comma-separated values (operators with shell vars
could trip on embedded commas), --actors plural alias (adds
surface for one concept), reject duplicate values at CLI
(substrate doesn't need dedup; PG handles duplicates fine).
Drawbacks — breaking field rename across three adapter input
types + JSON envelope (contained scope no external consumers
documented one-shot break), retention history actorId stays
single divergence with diff-timeline (intentional documented
future-Q if demand emerges), OR semantic only (substrate has
one actor per row AND semantic would need different data
model out of scope), no actor deduplication (PG handles
duplicates fine substrate doesn't need to dedup), no null-actor
sentinel (operators jq-filter for system events same as
ADR-0193 deferred decision), no CLI-side UUID validation
(matches ADR-0193 deferred). Adapter tests in trace-retention.
test.ts — the actor-filter describe block was rewritten with
breaking-rename in mind, expanded from 7 to 11 tests: pair-
wise IN($N) for single, pair-wise multi-actor IN($N1, $N2)
verifying OR semantic, pair-wise omits when not set, pair-wise
omits when empty array (treats as filter-not-set), N-way IN
single/multi positioned after tenant IN list, cross-table IN
single/multi positioned after table IN list, pair-wise composes
actorIds with --since + --until + --limit + joinActor, N-way
omits, cross-table omits — verifies the full SQL substring +
params expectations under the new field name. CLI tests in
retention.test.ts — rewrote the --actor-id describe block
expanded from 8 to 11 tests: pair-wise threads single as
[ACTOR_A], multi-actor threads as [ACTOR_A, ACTOR_B] via two
--actor-id flag occurrences, pair-wise omits actorIds when not
set backward compat, N-way + multi-actor threads alongside
--add-tenant, cross-table + multi-actor alongside --cross-
table, composes with --with-actor-names + --since + --limit,
JSON envelope echoes actorIds: [ACTOR_A] single, actorIds:
[ACTOR_A, ACTOR_B] multi, actorIds=null when flag NOT set,
JSON envelope on N-way + cross-table paths echoes actorIds
array. Test count: 8,809 → 8,815 (+6 net: adapter +4, CLI +2).
cli.ts helpText extended on both diff-timeline usage lines —
--actor-id flag is now described as repeatable with OR-semantic
multi-actor filter wording. ADR-0199 documents the design + 8
rejected alternatives + 7 future Qs (widen retention history's
actorId to actorIds for consistency, --actor-id-not exclusion,
--actor-name-equals repeated via meta.users JOIN, multi-actor
+ --system-only mode for null actor_id, composite index on
(actor_id, occurred_at), --actor-id @file.txt reading from
file, range cursor + multi-actor composition noted as already-
correct no-action-needed). The retention CLI now has 18 actions
with multi-actor filter on the diff-timeline surface — operators
get OR-semantic actor cohorts across pair-wise + N-way + cross-
table dispatch paths uniformly. Retention history's actor
filter stays single value intentionally; divergence documented.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.kind-filter
closes ADR-0194 Q6 by threading `--kind <event-kind>` through
`retention diff-history` as an expectation check (not a filter,
since diff-history takes exactly two IDs not a list query).
Adapter loads both events as before, then if eventKind is set
validates entryA.event_kind === input.eventKind AND
entryB.event_kind === input.eventKind, throwing with explicit
error naming the offending side(s) on mismatch — error message
'expected both events to have event_kind <X> but A is <Y> and
B is <Z>' depending on which side(s) violate. CLI validates
--kind against the 4-value OPT_OUT_HISTORY_EVENT_KINDS tuple
from ADR-0170 (invalid value exits 2 with explicit valid-
values list mirroring ADR-0194 pattern); valid value but
mismatch exits 1 (runtime path matches existing cross-tenant/
cross-table mismatch error propagation in diff-history).
DiffHistoryEntriesInput gains optional eventKind?:
OptOutHistoryEventKind field. JSON envelope gains kind:
OptOutHistoryEventKind | null field echoing operator's
expectation matches ADR-0194 envelope convention. No human-
format change — formatHistoryDiff already renders eventKindA +
eventKindB in metadata header so when --kind set and both
match operators visually verify expectation was met; when
kinds mismatch adapter throws before formatter is reached.
Use cases unblocked — compliance script asserting kind match
('--kind opt_out_set' diff fails when accidentally comparing
across kinds), forensic analysis gated on kind, CI gate
verifying migration script behavior ('--kind retention_set'
exits 1 if migration accidentally fired different mutation
kind). Why expectation-check not filter — diff-history takes
exactly two IDs nothing to filter; flag semantic shifts from
filter to expectation check; matches substrate's existing
validation-throwing pattern for cross-tenant + cross-table
mismatches. Rejected alternatives — --kind as filter limiting
fieldDiffs (doesn't match per-event semantic of event_kind
which is row-level not field-level), make mismatch warn not
throw (silent gates lose safety property), two flags --kind-a
+ --kind-b for per-side expectations (overkill for v1 defer),
validate but operator doesn't specify which (substrate can't
auto-pick), PG side validation only (PG can't know operator's
expectation), type adapter input as string (loses TypeScript
narrowing matches ADR-0194), JSON envelope field named
expectedKind (verbose; kind matches ADR-0194 convention),
render mismatch as fieldDiff (operators wouldn't expect to
see event_kind mismatch in fieldDiffs throwing is loud and
clear). Drawbacks — semantic shift from filter to expectation
check operators familiar with --kind elsewhere may expect
filter semantics (mitigated by adapter error message which
is explicit about expectation check), no per-event expectation
(operators with different per-side expectations jq-filter
JSON defer), single-kind only no multi-value tuple (operators
run multiple commands defer), two adapter errors with similar
shape (same-tenant/same-table mismatch + --kind mismatch
acceptable structural consistency). 5 new adapter tests in
trace-retention.test.ts in a new "PostgresTraceRetention.
diffHistoryEntries --kind expectation check" describe block
— accepts when both events have expected kind, throws when
event A's kind doesn't match expected with explicit error
naming side A + actual kind, throws when event B's kind
doesn't match with side B in error, throws naming both sides
when neither matches expected, omits the check when
eventKind not set backward compat verified. 6 new CLI tests
in retention.test.ts in a new "runRetention diff-history
--kind" describe block — exit 2 when --kind is invalid
value with explicit valid-values list, threads eventKind to
adapter when --kind set, omits eventKind when --kind NOT
set backward compat, adapter mismatch error propagates exit
1 with explicit error including expected + actual kinds,
JSON envelope echoes kind field when --kind set with
action:"diff-history", JSON envelope kind=null when --kind
NOT set. cli.ts helpText extended for retention diff-history
usage line with [--kind <event-kind>] flag + 3-line
description explaining expectation-check semantic + 4 valid
event_kind values + mismatch-exits-1 contract. ADR-0198
documents the design + 8 rejected alternatives + 6 future
Qs (per-side --kind-a + --kind-b for asymmetric
expectations, --kind <a>|<b>|<c> multi-value expectation,
--actor-id expectation check on diff-history same shape,
--actor-id + --kind composition for fine-grained checks,
--with-actor-names on diff-history LEFT JOIN for the two
events' actors, inline diff annotation showing when events
are different kinds without throwing for operators who
want diff anyway opt-in via separate flag). The retention
CLI now has 18 actions with --kind support uniform across
THREE retention diff family members — retention history
(filter from ADR-0170), retention diff-timeline (filter
from ADR-0194 across all three paths), retention diff-
history (expectation check from this milestone) — semantics
adapt to the surface (list-style filter vs cross-event
expectation check) but the flag name + 4-value tuple +
helpText valid-values list are consistent across the
family.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-timeline.before-id
closes ADR-0195 Q1 + ADR-0196 Q1 by adding `--before-id <uuid>`
reverse cursor pagination to all three `retention diff-timeline`
dispatch paths uniformly — pair-wise cross-tenant + N-way cross-
tenant via --add-tenant + cross-table via --cross-table. Brings
diff-timeline to bidirectional-pagination parity with retention
history (ADR-0196 just shipped --before-id there). Cursor SQL
flips operator from ADR-0195's `>` to `<` since diff-timeline
orders ASC (oldest first) and walking backward in time toward
older entries means going UP the list — `(h.occurred_at, h.id) <
(cursor)` returns earlier-timestamp entries. Mirror table: for
retention history (DESC), --after-id uses `<` (walk-down = older)
and --before-id uses `>` (walk-up = newer); for diff-timeline
(ASC), --after-id uses `>` (walk-down = newer) and --before-id
uses `<` (walk-up = older). Operator semantics consistent across
surfaces: --after-id always means "next chronologically-forward
page" and --before-id always means "previous chronologically-
backward page" regardless of underlying SQL operator. All three
diff-timeline input types gain optional beforeId?: string field;
all three adapter methods add identical conditional WHERE clause
when set positioned right after the afterId block before
params.push(limit). Adapter accepts both afterId + beforeId
simultaneously (substrate-level range-cursor semantic preserved
across all three diff-timeline paths matching ADR-0196 history
pattern) while CLI enforces mutual exclusivity at boundary —
both flags set returns exit 2 with clear error. CLI dispatcher
in runRetentionDiffTimeline reads both flags via getStringFlag,
checks mutual exclusivity BEFORE reading positionals (matches
ADR-0192's flag-validation-first pattern), threads beforeId
through to whichever of the three adapter methods is selected.
nextBeforeId computed at CLI layer for all three paths as the
FIRST entry's id when entries.length === limit (boundary for
going-older in ASC ordering — first entry is oldest); when
length < limit no more older entries → null. JSON envelope
gains beforeId: string | null + nextBeforeId: string | null
fields across all three envelope shapes (pair-wise no-
discriminator, nway:true, crossTable:true) alongside existing
afterId + nextAfterId from ADR-0195. Human-format hint
'Page full — previous page: crossengin retention diff-timeline
--before-id <id> ...' appended when nextBeforeId !== null;
both forward + backward hints can appear in same output when
both cursors are populated mirroring ADR-0196. Use cases
unblocked — page reversal on the timeline surface after
overshooting with --after-id, anchor-based bidirectional audit
on cohort timeline (--after-id <X> + --before-id <X> two
queries one each direction with --add-tenant), cross-table
backward audit walking back through multi-table timeline to
find when policies were originally established, resume from
saved cursor in either direction. Rejected alternatives —
reverse ORDER BY on --before-id (would confuse operators
expecting ASC matches ADR-0189/0195 consistent ordering),
reject both flags at adapter boundary (overzealous substrate-
level range-cursor is sound), single --cursor + --direction
flag (verbose explicit named flags clearer matches ADR-0196/
0195/0175 precedent), use LAST entry id for nextBeforeId
(would point at newest entry useless for backward toward-
older pagination), compute nextBeforeId only when --before-id
explicitly set (would hide bidirectional cursors on first
page; both cursors every full page matches ADR-0196 convention),
auto-paginate via streaming generator (operators want explicit
control), nextBeforeId named prevBeforeId in envelope
(inconsistent with nextAfterId), two separate adapter methods
per direction (3 methods × 2 directions = 6 structural
explosion). Drawbacks — three adapter methods each gain
identical cursor logic for the second time (after ADR-0195's
--after-id) structural symmetry mirrors established pattern
acceptable maintenance cost, adapter accepts both afterId +
beforeId simultaneously but CLI rejects substrate-flexibility/
CLI-restriction split documented, both nextAfterId +
nextBeforeId populated on every full page matches ADR-0196
convention (first-page nextBeforeId returns empty on follow-up
correct end-of-pagination signal in backward direction), no
CLI-side UUID validation matches deferred decision across
the cursor work, cursor operators flipped vs retention history
(`<` here vs `>` on history for --before-id) operators reading
both surfaces may find inversion mildly confusing (mitigated
by consistent semantic labeling regardless of underlying SQL
operator). 9 new adapter tests in trace-retention.test.ts in
a new "PostgresTraceRetention diff-timeline --before-id reverse
cursor" describe block — pair-wise --before-id threads as $N
param into compound cursor with < operator (ASC walk-backward
toward older) verified via SQL substring + params, pair-wise
same $N reused for both subquery lookup and tiebreaker via
regex count appears twice, pair-wise omits when not set, N-way
threads positioned after tenant IN list + table param, cross-
table threads positioned after table IN list + tenant param,
pair-wise composes beforeId with afterId in SAME query both
cursor clauses present (range semantic — substrate-level
flexibility preserved), N-way omits when not set, cross-table
omits when not set, pair-wise ORDER BY remains h.occurred_at
ASC + h.id ASC no direction reversal. 12 new CLI tests in
retention.test.ts in a new "runRetention diff-timeline
--before-id" describe block — pair-wise threads beforeId to
adapter, pair-wise omits when not set backward compat verified,
exit 2 when --after-id + --before-id both set mutually
exclusive with explicit error message, N-way threads alongside
--add-tenant, cross-table threads alongside --cross-table,
JSON envelope echoes beforeId when set (pair-wise), JSON
envelope beforeId=null when not set, JSON nextBeforeId is
FIRST entry id when length === limit verifying ASC ordering
oldest-first (with nextAfterId verifying both populated and
pointing at correct boundaries), JSON nextBeforeId=null when
length < limit, human-format prints previous-page hint with
--before-id <id> when length === limit, JSON envelope echoes
beforeId + nextBeforeId on N-way path, JSON envelope echoes
beforeId + nextBeforeId on cross-table path. cli.ts helpText
extended with [--before-id <uuid>] flag note on both diff-
timeline usage lines + mutual-exclusivity note + nextAfterId
+ nextBeforeId bidirectional navigation. ADR-0197 documents
the design + 8 rejected alternatives + 7 future Qs (--range
range-cursor flag at CLI boundary leveraging adapter's both-
cursors-set support same Q as ADR-0196 Q2, --before-id on
retention diff-history, cross-process cursor stability,
--watch flag for stream-tail mode, --include-cursor-row for
>=/<= semantic, composite indexes for scale, unified
pagination flag set across surfaces noted as no-action-needed
since flag names already unify). The retention CLI now has
18 actions with bidirectional cursor pagination uniform
across BOTH audit-log surfaces — retention history
--after-id + --before-id from ADR-0175 + ADR-0196 (DESC)
and retention diff-timeline --after-id + --before-id from
ADR-0195 + this milestone (ASC across all three dispatch
paths) — operators have stable forward + backward cursor-
paginated workflows on every audit-log query in the retention
substrate without OFFSET drift under concurrent inserts.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.history.before-id
closes ADR-0175 Q1 by adding `--before-id <uuid>` reverse cursor
pagination to `retention history` — walking BACKWARD through the
DESC-ordered audit log toward newer entries. After ADR-0175
shipped `--after-id` forward pagination (DESC ordering walks
backward in time toward older entries) and ADR-0195 just shipped
`--after-id` across all three diff-timeline paths, operators now
have genuine bidirectional pagination needs — resume from saved
cursor in either direction, page reversal after overshooting,
sliding-window audit anchored on a specific event id, stream-
tail polling for newer events. CLI surface — `--after-id` and
`--before-id` are mutually exclusive at the CLI boundary (both
set → exit 2 with clear error); both compose with every existing
filter (--tenant + --table + --kind + --actor-id +
--with-actor-names + --since + --until + --limit). Adapter
ListOptOutHistoryInput gains optional beforeId?: string field;
adapter accepts BOTH afterId and beforeId simultaneously
(substrate-level range-cursor semantic preserved for
programmatic consumers) while CLI rejects both — documented
flexibility/restriction split. SQL — compound cursor
`(h.occurred_at, h.id) > ((SELECT occurred_at FROM ... WHERE
id = $N), $N)` flips ADR-0175's `<` operator since walking
forward (up the DESC list) means returning NEWER entries; same
inline subquery + tiebreaker pattern. ORDER BY remains
unchanged at `h.occurred_at DESC, h.id DESC` regardless of
cursor direction — operators see latest entries first in every
page. nextBeforeId computed at CLI layer as the FIRST entry's
id when entries.length === limit (boundary for going-newer in
DESC ordering); when entries.length < limit, no more newer
entries exist nextBeforeId is null. JSON envelope gains
beforeId: string | null + nextBeforeId: string | null fields
alongside existing afterId + nextAfterId. Human-format adds
'Page full — previous page: crossengin retention history
--before-id <id> ...' hint when nextBeforeId !== null. Both
cursor fields can be populated when page is full — operators
navigate either direction from any page; on first-page queries
the nextBeforeId points at the absolute newest entry and
follow-up --before-id <that> returns empty (correct end-of-
pagination signal in backward direction); matches GitHub REST
Link-header convention. Use cases unblocked — page reversal
after walking back too far (--before-id <first-id-from-current-
page>), stream-tail mode (polling --before-id <latest-known>
every 30s discovers new events without re-scanning), sliding-
window audit anchored on a specific event id (two queries one
each direction), resume from saved cursor in either direction.
Rejected alternatives — flip ORDER BY direction on --before-id
(would confuse operators expecting DESC; single consistent
ORDER BY matches convention), reject both --after-id and
--before-id at adapter boundary (overzealous substrate-level
range-cursor is sound), single --cursor + --direction flag
(verbose explicit named flags clearer), nextBeforeId named
prevBeforeId (abbreviates inconsistently with nextAfterId), use
LAST entry id for nextBeforeId (would point at oldest entry
useless for backward pagination), compute nextBeforeId only
when --before-id explicitly set (would require operators know
direction availability before trying), reverse-direction
reverse-mode with --before-id + ASC ordering (semantic stretch
DESC is expected), server-side opaque cursor encoding (UUID is
simple). Drawbacks — adapter accepts both flags simultaneously
but CLI rejects (substrate-flexibility/CLI-restriction split
documented), both nextAfterId + nextBeforeId populated on every
full page (first-page nextBeforeId points at absolute newest
entry follow-up returns empty as end-of-pagination signal), no
--before-id on retention diff-timeline this milestone (defer
to future session), no CLI-side UUID validation (matches
ADR-0175/0186/0193/0195 deferred decision), nextBeforeId best-
effort under concurrent inserts (walked correctly on next
backward page via compound cursor), DESC ordering constant
regardless of cursor direction (matches GitHub REST and most
paginated APIs convention). 6 new adapter tests in trace-
retention.test.ts in a new "PostgresTraceRetention.listOptOutHistory
--before-id reverse cursor" describe block — threads as $N
param into compound cursor with > operator verified, same $N
reused for both subquery lookup and tiebreaker verified via
regex count appears twice, omits when not set, composes with
afterId in SAME query both cursor clauses present + both
params threaded (range semantic), composes with all 7 other
filters with afterId positioned before beforeId in WHERE
clause order, ORDER BY remains h.occurred_at DESC + h.id DESC
no direction reversal. 10 new CLI tests in retention.test.ts
in a new "runRetention history --before-id" describe block —
threads beforeId to adapter, omits when not set backward
compat, exit 2 when --after-id + --before-id both set mutually
exclusive with explicit error, JSON envelope echoes beforeId
when set, JSON envelope beforeId=null when not set, JSON
nextBeforeId is FIRST entry id when length === limit (with
nextAfterId verifying both populated), JSON nextBeforeId=null
when length < limit, human-format prints previous-page hint
when length === limit, human-format omits hint when length <
limit, composes with all 7 other filters (--tenant + --table
+ --kind + --actor-id + --since + --until + --before-id +
--limit). cli.ts helpText extended with [--before-id <uuid>]
flag note on retention history usage line + 5-line description
explaining bidirectional pagination + mutual exclusivity with
--after-id + nextAfterId + nextBeforeId bidirectional
navigation when page is full. ADR-0196 documents the design +
8 rejected alternatives + 7 future Qs (--before-id on retention
diff-timeline all three paths, --range <after-id>..<before-id>
range-cursor flag at CLI boundary leveraging adapter's both-
cursors-set support, --before-id on retention diff-history,
cross-process cursor stability via point-in-time snapshot,
--watch flag auto-polling for stream-tail mode, --include-
cursor-row for >=/<= semantic, composite indexes). The
retention CLI now has 18 actions with bidirectional cursor
pagination on the canonical audit-log surface — operators
walk forward (--after-id, older) and backward (--before-id,
newer) on retention history symmetrically without OFFSET
drift under concurrent inserts; diff-timeline still has
forward-only --after-id from ADR-0195 with --before-id
deferred to future session.
M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-timeline.cursor
closes ADR-0189 Q5 by threading `--after-id <uuid>` cursor
pagination through all three `retention diff-timeline` dispatch
paths uniformly — pair-wise cross-tenant + N-way cross-tenant via
--add-tenant + cross-table via --cross-table. Mirrors ADR-0175's
compound-cursor pattern from retention history but with `>`
operator instead of `<` because diff-timeline orders ASC (walk-
forward in time) vs history's DESC (walk-backward). Compound
`(h.occurred_at, h.id) > ((SELECT occurred_at FROM ... WHERE id =
$N), $N)` cursor handles ties under shared occurred_at via UUID
v7 id tiebreaker — same $N param reused for both inline subquery
lookup and tiebreaker so operators don't need to pass both id
AND occurred_at, substrate resolves timestamp server-side from
the id. All three input types gain optional afterId?: string
field; all three adapter methods add identical conditional WHERE
clause when set positioned after --since/--until before limit
param push. nextAfterId computed at CLI layer (mirrors ADR-0175)
— when entries.length === limit, last entry's id; else null;
best-effort (concurrent inserts may add events later walked
correctly on next page). JSON envelope echoes afterId: string |
null + nextAfterId: string | null across all three envelope
shapes. Human-format appends 'Page full — next page: crossengin
retention diff-timeline --after-id <uuid> ...' hint when
nextAfterId !== null; omitted when entries.length < limit.
Composes with every existing flag (--actor-id, --kind, --with-
actor-names, --add-tenant, --cross-table, --add-table, --since,
--until, --limit) without restriction. Use cases unblocked —
iterate through >100-event audit window via --limit + nextAfterId
follow-up, streaming cohort audit via bash while-loop chaining
--after-id, resume interrupted audit from last seen id, per-actor
per-kind paginated forensics combining all filters with cursor.
Rejected alternatives — OFFSET-based pagination via --page N
(unstable under concurrent inserts same as ADR-0175 rejection),
two-cursor --after-id + --before-id (single forward direction
covers common case defer bidirectional), single-key occurred_at
cursor (ties cause skipped pages), single-key id cursor (UUID v7
mostly time-ordered but not strictly), totalAvailable count in
envelope (separate COUNT(*) round-trip per page defer), validate
--after-id UUID at CLI boundary (PG enforces with clearer error
matches ADR-0175/0186/0193), auto-paginate via streaming
generator (operators want explicit page control shell loops
cover), nextAfterId always populated even when entries.length <
limit (would mislead operators), server-side opaque cursor
