control-plane enumeration; fine-tune / continued-pretrain /
distillation inventory; 8-parameter filter set is the largest
yet), ADR-0116 covers M2.X.5.aa.z.14 (Bedrock getCustomModel
with training/validation detail — third extended-shape detail
instance; training/validation/output provenance + metrics +
hyperparameters + distillation lineage all surfaced),

ADR-0161 covers M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.reason

ADR-0162 covers M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.expiry

tenant_opt_out variant gains optOutUntil; SQL NOT IN

(opt_out AND (opt_out_until IS NULL OR opt_out_until > now())));

changes; self-managing legal holds + calendar-driven
expirations + reduced compliance theater),

computed at CLI layer as FIRST entry id when

JSON envelope echoes beforeId + nextBeforeId;
human-format adds 'Page full — previous page:
--before-id <id>' hint; page reversal + stream-
tail polling + sliding-window audit + resume from
saved cursor either direction workflows now CLI-
native),

ADR-0204 covers M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.with-actor-names
(`retention diff-history --with-actor-names` actor
display name surfacing — closes ADR-0203 Q3 +
ADR-0198 Q5; composes ADR-0185's LEFT JOIN
meta.users infrastructure with diff-history surface;
DiffHistoryEntriesInput gains joinActor?: boolean;
when set adapter widens SELECT to include
u.display_name + u.email via LEFT JOIN on actor_id;
result type gains required actorIdA + actorIdB
fields (always populated from existing actor_id
SELECT column since ADR-0203) and optional
actorDisplayNameA/B + actorEmailA/B fields populated
only when joinActor=true; SQL aliased h. consistently
matching ADR-0185/0190 pattern; CLI threads
--with-actor-names via getBooleanFlag passes
joinActor to adapter passes opts.withActorNames to
formatHistoryDiff which appends 'by <actor>' suffix
on each event line using generalized formatActor
helper from ADR-0190 (display_name (uuid) → email
(uuid) → raw uuid → <system> for null actor_id);
composes with --kind (ADR-0198) + --actor-id
(ADR-0203) without restriction; JSON envelope gains
withActorNames discriminator + result conditionally
carries display fields when set; minor breaking
change in DiffHistoryEntriesResult — actorIdA +
actorIdB now required where they were absent in
ADR-0173 (contained scope reflects always-available
data); use cases unblocked — one-command "who made
each change" diff audit, compliance review with
named actor attribution, compose with --actor-id +
--with-actor-names for forensic discipline, incident
post-mortems with named actor timelines; 8 new
adapter tests in a new "PostgresTraceRetention.diffHistoryEntries
--with-actor-names" describe block (omits JOIN
backward compat, emits LEFT JOIN meta.users with
display_name + email cols, returns actor info both
events, returns null for orphan FK, null for system
actor, omits display fields when joinActor false,
always populates actorIdA + actorIdB even without
join, composes with --actor-id expectation check);
9 new CLI tests in a new "runRetention diff-history
--with-actor-names" describe block (threads
joinActor when --with-actor-names set, omits
joinActor backward compat, human-format renders 'by
Name (uuid)' for both events, <system> for null
actor_id, email fallback when display_name null,
omits 'by ...' suffix when flag NOT set, JSON
envelope withActorNames + actor fields when set,
JSON withActorNames=false when NOT set, composes
with --actor-id + --kind); cli.ts helpText extended
with [--with-actor-names] flag note + 3-line
description; future Qs cover --actor-name-equals
filter, show user status, display tenant_membership
role, per-field actor attribution, --actor-id-not
exclusion to compose with display, apply pattern
uniformly to other diff-* surfaces. The retention
CLI now has 18 actions with --with-actor-names
support uniform across THREE retention audit-log
surfaces — retention history (ADR-0185), retention
diff-timeline (ADR-0190 across all three paths),
retention diff-history (this milestone) — operators
get human-readable actor attribution uniformly
across single-tenant audit-log + cross-tenant
timeline diff + cross-event policy diff workflows).
ADR-0205 covers M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.actor-not
(`retention diff-history --actor-id-not <uuid>` actor
exclusion expectation check — closes ADR-0204 Q5 +
ADR-0203 Q1 in spirit; inverse of ADR-0203's positive
--actor-id expectation; operator declares "I expect
neither event to have actor_id X" and adapter throws
when reality differs; DiffHistoryEntriesInput gains
optional actorIdNot?: string field; adapter exclusion
check fires after the actorId expectation check with
error message naming offending side(s) compactly —
single match: "but A matches", both match: "but both
A and B match"; asymmetric rendering vs --actor-id
positive check (shows actual value) because operator
already knows the excluded value naming the side
suffices; null actor_id correctly passes when
--actor-id-not is a UUID (null !== UUID); CLI threads
via getStringFlag, echoes actorIdNot: string | null
in JSON envelope; NOT mutually exclusive with
--actor-id at CLI boundary operators compose freely;
contradictory --actor-id X --actor-id-not X surfaces
actorId-check error first; mismatch exits 1; no CLI-
side UUID validation matches deferred decision; use
cases — tier-migration anti-actor verification,
suspended-user audit pre-check, CI gate excluding
automation, "this isn't me" forensic discipline; 8
new adapter tests (accepts when neither matches,
accepts when both null and excluded is UUID, throws
A matches, throws B matches, throws both A and B
match, omits check backward compat, composes with
--actor-id distinct passes, contradictory composition
surfaces actorId-check first); 6 new CLI tests
(threads actorIdNot, omits backward compat, composes
with --actor-id independently threaded, adapter error
propagates exit 1, JSON envelope echoes when set,
JSON null when NOT set); cli.ts helpText extended
with [--actor-id-not <uuid>] flag note + 2-line
description; future Qs cover --actor-id-not multi-
value tuple, per-side --actor-id-not-a + --actor-id-
not-b, --system-only + --no-system explicit null
matching, --kind-not exclusion companion, --actor-id-
not filter on diff-timeline different semantic,
--actor-id-not filter on retention history. The
retention diff-history surface now has both positive
(--actor-id from ADR-0203) and negative (--actor-id-
not from this milestone) expectation checks composing
with event-kind expectation check (--kind from ADR-
0198) + actor display surfacing (--with-actor-names
from ADR-0204) — operators get full positive +
negative expectation gates with named-actor audit
readability uniformly on the cross-event policy diff
surface).
ADR-0206 covers M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.history.actor-not
(`retention history --actor-id-not <uuid>` substrate-
side WHERE NOT filter — closes ADR-0186 Q1 + ADR-0205
Q6; inverse of ADR-0186's positive --actor-id filter
on list-style audit-log surface; different semantic
from ADR-0205's exclusion expectation check on diff-
history (which throws on match in 2-fixed-event
compare) — this is a list-style query filter
excluding rows where actor_id matches; ListOptOutHistoryInput
gains optional actorIdNot?: string field; SQL adds
`(h.actor_id IS NULL OR h.actor_id != $N)` WHERE
clause explicitly OR'd IS NULL so system events
INCLUDED (PG actor_id != $N returns NULL for null
values silently filtering systems out which is wrong
intent); substrate-side filter for LIMIT correctness
+ cursor-pagination correctness + index usage parity;
CLI threads via getStringFlag, echoes actorIdNot:
string | null in JSON envelope; NOT mutually exclusive
with --actor-id at CLI boundary; contradictory
--actor-id X --actor-id-not X returns empty silently
(natural outcome); no CLI-side UUID validation; use
cases — service-account-free human audit, suspended-
user activity audit, self-exclude review, compliance
non-automation filter; 8 new adapter tests (adds
WHERE clause with IS NULL OR != verified, omits when
not set, includes system events (null actor_id),
composes with tenantId + actorId both threaded though
SQL-contradictory + joinActor + all-filter-dimensions,
returns rows excluding the actor); 6 new CLI tests
(threads actorIdNot, omits backward compat, composes
with --actor-id independently, composes with --tenant
+ --with-actor-names + --actor-id-not 3-flag, JSON
envelope echoes when set, JSON null when NOT set);
cli.ts helpText extended with [--actor-id-not <uuid>]
flag note + 2-line description; future Qs cover
multi-value via repeated flag using multiFlags from
ADR-0183, --system-only + --no-system explicit null
matching, --actor-id-not on diff-timeline, --kind-not
companion, composite index for scale, --actor-name-
not via meta.users JOIN. The retention CLI now has
18 actions with --actor-id-not support spanning TWO
retention audit-log surfaces — retention diff-history
(expectation check from ADR-0205) and retention
history (substrate-side filter from this milestone)
— operators get full positive + negative actor
expectations across both cross-event and list-style
query surfaces with surface-appropriate semantics
under one consistent flag name).
ADR-0207 covers M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-timeline.actor-not
(`retention diff-timeline --actor-id-not <uuid>`
repeatable multi-value exclusion filter across all
three diff-timeline dispatch paths — closes ADR-0193
+ ADR-0206 Q3; mirror of ADR-0206's retention-history
pattern on diff-timeline surface but multi-value to
match ADR-0199's positive multi-actor filter shape on
diff-timeline (within-surface consistency wins over
cross-surface uniformity); all three input types gain
actorIdsNot?: ReadonlyArray<string>; SQL adds
(h.actor_id IS NULL OR h.actor_id NOT IN ($N1, $N2,
...)) when set non-empty explicitly OR'd IS NULL so
system events INCLUDED; empty array filter-not-set
matching ADR-0199 convention; CLI getMultiFlag
("actor-id-not") repeated flag occurrences threaded
to whichever of three adapters dispatched; JSON
envelope echoes actorIdsNot: string[] | null on all
three envelope shapes positioned after actorIds field;
NOT mutually exclusive with --actor-id both clauses
fire independently at SQL layer contradictory returns
empty silently natural outcome; parallel implementation
across three paths matches established pattern; use
cases unblocked — cohort drift detection excluding
noise actors, cross-table audit excluding automation,
self-exclude reviewer audit, compose positive +
negative for overlapping cohorts; 9 new adapter tests
(pair-wise single + multi NOT IN, omits clause when
not set, empty array filter-not-set, N-way + cross-
table positioned correctly, composes with actorIds +
joinActor + eventKinds); 10 new CLI tests (pair-wise
single + multi threading, omits backward compat, N-way
+ cross-table threading, composes with --actor-id all
three JSON envelopes echo actorIdsNot pair-wise null
when NOT set); cli.ts helpText extended on both diff-
timeline usage lines; future Qs cover --actor-id-not
on diff-history already covered as expectation check
from ADR-0205, widen retention history to multi-value
pairs with ADR-0206 Q1, --system-only + --no-system
explicit null actor matching, --kind-not exclusion
companion, composite index for scale, --actor-name-
not filter via meta.users JOIN. The retention CLI now
has 18 actions with --actor-id-not support spanning
THREE retention audit-log surfaces — retention history
(substrate-side filter from ADR-0206), retention diff-
history (expectation check from ADR-0205), retention
diff-timeline (substrate-side multi-value filter
across all 3 paths from this milestone) — operators
get full positive + negative actor expectations
across all audit-log surfaces with surface-
appropriate semantics under one consistent flag name).
ADR-0208 covers M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.kind-not
(`crossengin retention diff-history --kind-not <event-
kind>` event-kind exclusion expectation check — closes
ADR-0205 Q4 + ADR-0198 Q5 in spirit; inverse of
ADR-0198's positive --kind expectation and mirror of
ADR-0205's --actor-id-not on the event-kind dimension;
completes the positive/negative × kind/actor expectation
matrix on the diff-history surface — --kind (ADR-0198)
+ --kind-not (this milestone) + --actor-id (ADR-0203) +
--actor-id-not (ADR-0205) + --with-actor-names
(ADR-0204) form 5-flag family across both row-level
dimensions plus actor display surfacing; operator
declares "I expect neither event to have event_kind X"
and adapter throws when reality differs;
DiffHistoryEntriesInput gains optional eventKindNot?:
OptOutHistoryEventKind field; adapter exclusion check
fires after the eventKind expectation check (positioned
immediately after --kind so kind-dimension checks group
together before actor-dimension checks) with error
message naming offending side(s) compactly — single
match: "but A matches", both match: "but both A and B
match"; asymmetric rendering vs --kind positive check
(which shows actual value) because operator already
knows the excluded value, naming the side suffices;
identical compact-error-message shape as ADR-0205
--actor-id-not for consistency across the family; CLI
threads --kind-not via getStringFlag, validates via
isOptOutHistoryEventKind against 4-value
OPT_OUT_HISTORY_EVENT_KINDS tuple from ADR-0170 (invalid
value exits 2 with explicit valid-values list mirroring
ADR-0194 + ADR-0198 patterns), passes eventKindNot to
adapter, echoes kindNot: OptOutHistoryEventKind | null
in JSON envelope; NOT mutually exclusive with --kind at
CLI boundary — operators compose freely (both must be
opt_out_set AND neither is policy_deleted is valid +
redundant; contradictory --kind X --kind-not X surfaces
the kind-check error first since order matters; both
error messages are clear); mismatch exits 1 (runtime
path matching ADR-0198 + ADR-0203 + ADR-0205 + cross-
tenant/cross-table mismatch propagation); use cases —
anti-deletion verification (assert neither event is
policy_deleted), compliance gate excluding system-driven
kinds (assert both are opt_out_* not retention_set
automation), CI gate on tier migration (this diff is
opt-out lifecycle not migration noise), forensic
disambiguation (opt-out toggles not policy deletion);
rejected alternatives — --kind-not as filter limiting
fieldDiffs (doesn't fit diff-history takes 2 fixed IDs
not list query), make exclusion match warn not throw
(silent gates lose safety), two flags --kind-not-a +
--kind-not-b per-side (overkill defer), CLI-enforced
mutual exclusivity with --kind (would block valid
compositions like "both must be opt_out_set AND
neither is policy_deleted"; adapter-level errors
surface contradictions naturally), render mismatch as
fieldDiff (event_kind isn't a field in next_state JSONB;
throwing is loud), PG WHERE NOT silent zero rows
(indistinguishable from no-match throw clearer), use
IN/NOT IN SQL multi-value (overkill substrate single-
value matches ADR-0205 single-value pattern), inverse
flag named --exclude-kind (verbose --kind-not matches
ADR-0205 naming convention); drawbacks — single-value
exclusion only (multi-value defer; the 4-value tuple
makes multi-exclusion less compelling than multi-actor
since operators can just use --kind <complement>),
composition with --kind can be contradictory (clear
adapter error in either ordering), CLI-side validation
requires exact match (operators with typos hit exit 2
matching ADR-0198 pattern), asymmetric error rendering
vs --kind positive check (different rendering reflects
different info needs matches ADR-0205 stance), no per-
side --kind-not-a + --kind-not-b (asymmetric exclusion
defer pairs with ADR-0203 Q1 + ADR-0205 Q2), no
semantic-shape exclusion (operators wanting "neither
event may be a write-mutation" can't express without
enumerating each kind; defer different surface), one
more flag to remember on diff-history (5 optional flags
documented via 4 separate description paragraphs in
helpText); 8 new adapter tests in a new
"PostgresTraceRetention.diffHistoryEntries --kind-not
exclusion check" describe block — accepts when neither
event has the excluded event_kind, throws when A
matches with explicit "A matches" error, throws when B
matches with "B matches", throws naming both when both
match with "both A and B match", omits the check when
eventKindNot not set backward compat, composes with
--kind expectation check when distinct (both pass),
contradictory --kind + --kind-not surfaces kind check
first, composes with --actor-id-not (both checks fire
independently — null actors + UUID exclusion + kind
exclusion); 7 new CLI tests in a new "runRetention
diff-history --kind-not" describe block — invalid
--kind-not value exit 2 with explicit valid-values list,
threads eventKindNot to adapter when --kind-not set,
omits eventKindNot when --kind-not NOT set backward
compat, composes with --kind threading both eventKind
and eventKindNot, adapter exclusion error propagates
exit 1 with explicit error including expected +
offending side, JSON envelope echoes kindNot when set,
JSON envelope kindNot=null when NOT set; cli.ts
helpText extended for retention diff-history usage line
with [--kind-not <event-kind>] flag (reorganized 3-
line flag layout to fit the 5-flag family) + 2-line
description explaining anti-kind verification semantic
+ mirror-of-`--kind` framing. The retention diff-
history surface now has THREE expectation-check flag
families forming a 2×2 + 1 matrix — kind dimension
(--kind positive from ADR-0198 + --kind-not negative
from this milestone) + actor dimension (--actor-id
positive from ADR-0203 + --actor-id-not negative from
ADR-0205) + actor display surfacing (--with-actor-
names from ADR-0204) — operators get full positive +
negative expectation gates across both row-level
dimensions with named-actor audit readability uniformly
on the cross-event policy diff surface; future Qs
cover --kind-not multi-value via comma-separated or
repeated flag using multiFlags from ADR-0183, per-
side --kind-not-a + --kind-not-b for asymmetric
exclusion pairing with ADR-0203 Q1 + ADR-0205 Q2,
--kind-not filter on retention history pairs with
ADR-0206 Q4 (which explicitly listed --kind-not
companion as future Q), --kind-not filter on diff-
timeline across all 3 paths matching ADR-0207 multi-
value pattern pairs with ADR-0207 Q4, semantic-shape
exclusion grouping write-mutations operator-policy
defer. The `--kind-not` family is now half-shipped on
the retention CLI — diff-history expectation check
from this milestone; retention history + diff-timeline
filter-side variants remain as ADR-0206 Q4 + ADR-0207
Q4 deferred future work following the established
--actor-id-not precedent from ADR-0205 + ADR-0206 +
ADR-0207).
ADR-0209 covers M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.history.system-only
(`crossengin retention history --system-only` /
`--no-system` boolean flag pair — closes ADR-0186 +
ADR-0206 + ADR-0205/0207 future Qs that listed this
across 4 retention ADRs; the retention history audit
log distinguishes two actor kinds — UUID-bearing rows
(human or service-account authored) and actor_id IS
NULL rows (system-initiated substrate background jobs
— prune sweeps, scheduled retention runs, automated
expiry lifts); operators have legitimate "show only X"
and "exclude all X" workflows for both kinds (system-
only audit for substrate-job health monitoring, no-
system audit for compliance attestation excluding
automation, forensic discipline during incidents,
human-only attestation for HIPAA/SOC 2/21 CFR 11);
until this milestone operators jq-filtered post-fetch
breaking LIMIT correctness + cursor-pagination
correctness + index-usage parity — substrate-side
WHERE the only way to get all three right (same
reasoning as ADR-0186 actor filter + ADR-0206 actor-
not filter); CLI surface `--system-only` boolean flag
filters to actor_id IS NULL events; `--no-system`
boolean flag filters to actor_id IS NOT NULL events;
mutually exclusive at CLI boundary both set returns
exit 2 with `--system-only and --no-system are
mutually exclusive` error before any PG query (CI logs
recognize as misuse); composes with every existing
filter without restriction; new exported
ActorPresenceFilter discriminated string union
`"system_only" | "no_system"` over two-boolean
adapter shape because (1) mutual exclusivity enforced
at type level — impossible to express "both at once"
in adapter input, (2) adapter consumers see clean
3-state choice, (3) CLI translates two boolean flags
into single union value after mutual-exclusivity
check; ListOptOutHistoryInput gains optional
actorPresence?: ActorPresenceFilter field; SQL adds
one of two conditional WHERE clauses NO PARAMS needed
(IS NULL / IS NOT NULL are SQL constructs not value
comparisons): system_only adds h.actor_id IS NULL;
no_system adds h.actor_id IS NOT NULL; position
immediately after actorIdNot filter clause before
--since/--until time-range clauses matching existing
actor-dimension grouping in WHERE-clause assembly;
JSON envelope gains two boolean fields systemOnly +
noSystem matching existing withActorNames boolean-
echo convention from ADR-0185 (every CLI flag has
corresponding envelope field with same shape;
operators read literal flag state; mutual exclusivity
at CLI guarantees both never true simultaneously);
composition with existing actor filters — --system-
only + --actor-id is contradictory at data layer
(system events have actor_id IS NULL; --actor-id
requires UUID; substrate returns empty silently
natural outcome), --system-only + --actor-id-not is
redundant but valid (system events are NEITHER any
specific UUID), --no-system + --actor-id is canonical
"human X's mutations" idiom (--no-system redundant
since actor_id = $N implies IS NOT NULL but
harmless), --no-system + --actor-id-not is canonical
"human mutations excluding X" idiom (distinguished
from --actor-id-not alone which includes system
events); use cases unblocked — system-authored
maintenance audit (count system events by kind for
dashboard), human-only compliance attestation
(quarterly audit excluding automation), forensic
incident scoping (was this state change human-
driven?), substrate-job health monitoring (count by
event_kind grouped), compose with actor exclusion
(human mutations excluding migration SA); rejected
alternatives — two separate boolean fields on
adapter (allows invalid both-true state), --actor-
presence single flag with enum value (operators
remember enum vocab vs natural boolean flags),
--system / --human flag names ("human" misleading
since SAs also non-system), --actor-id null sentinel
(overloads --actor-id UUID semantic), CLI-side
--actor-id X + --system-only contradiction error
(would block valid edge cases), substrate-side both-
flag mutual-exclusivity error in adapter (type union
makes invalid unrepresentable), two-value enum
--actor-presence required|excluded (operator vocab
unusual), PG partial index on actor_id IS NULL in
this milestone (premature optimization defer),
--system-only without --no-system companion
(operators wanting exclude system jq-filter post-
fetch with same LIMIT/pagination correctness problems;
ship both for symmetry), default --no-system=true
silent behavior change for existing callers backward-
incompatible; 8 new adapter tests in a new
"PostgresTraceRetention.listOptOutHistory
actorPresence filter" describe block — adds
h.actor_id IS NULL WHERE clause when system_only,
adds h.actor_id IS NOT NULL WHERE clause when
no_system, omits clause when not set, adds no params
for IS NULL/IS NOT NULL clauses no placeholder
pollution LIMIT param position correctly assigned,
composes with tenantId + tableName (system_only),
composes with actorIdNot + no_system (redundant but
valid both clauses present), composes with joinActor
+ actorPresence (LEFT JOIN + IS NULL both present),
returns rows with null actor_id when system_only
matches; 9 new CLI tests in a new "runRetention
history --system-only / --no-system" describe block
— exit 2 when both flags set with explicit error
message, threads actorPresence='system_only' to
adapter when --system-only set, threads
actorPresence='no_system' when --no-system set,
omits actorPresence when neither flag set backward
compat, composes with --tenant + --system-only,
composes with --actor-id-not + --no-system both
threaded independently, JSON envelope echoes
systemOnly=true + noSystem=false when --system-only
set, JSON envelope echoes noSystem=true +
systemOnly=false when --no-system set, JSON envelope
echoes both false when neither set; cli.ts helpText
extended for retention history usage line with
[--system-only | --no-system] notation indicating
mutual exclusivity + 3-line description added
explaining IS NULL / IS NOT NULL semantic + mutual
exclusivity. Future Qs cover --system-only/--no-
system on diff-history as expectation check ("both
events must be system" / "neither is system") pairing
with ADR-0205 Q3 deferred, --system-only/--no-system
on diff-timeline across all 3 dispatch paths pairing
with ADR-0193 + ADR-0207 future Qs deferred, partial
index on actor_id IS NULL for scale defer until
measured, short-circuit LEFT JOIN when
actorPresence='system_only' since system events have
no user row defer optimization, per-side
--system-only-a/--system-only-b on diff-history
defer pairs with deferred per-side --actor-id-a/
--actor-id-b from ADR-0203 Q1, --actor-id null
sentinel rejected for overload concerns. The
retention history surface now has FIVE actor-related
filter dimensions — specific actor positive
(--actor-id from ADR-0186) + specific actor negative
(--actor-id-not from ADR-0206) + system presence
positive (--system-only from this milestone) + system
presence negative (--no-system from this milestone)
+ actor display (--with-actor-names from ADR-0185)
— operators get first-class system-vs-human +
specific-actor inclusion/exclusion audit-log
workflows without LIMIT + pagination + index-usage
correctness problems of jq-filter workarounds. The
`--system-only` / `--no-system` family is now 1-of-3
surfaces: retention history (substrate-side filter
from this milestone); retention diff-history
(expectation check from ADR-0205 Q3 deferred);
retention diff-timeline (substrate-side filter
across all 3 paths from ADR-0207 Q3 + ADR-0193
future Q deferred). Subsequent milestones close the
remaining two surfaces mechanically following the
established per-surface precedent from --actor-id-
not (ADR-0205 expectation check + ADR-0206 history
filter + ADR-0207 diff-timeline multi-value filter).
The retention CLI now has 18 actions with --system-
only / --no-system support shipped on the retention
history surface).
ADR-0210 covers M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.history.actor-not.multi
(`crossengin retention history --actor-id-not <uuid>`
repeatable for multi-value OR-semantic exclusion
filter — closes ADR-0206 Q1 by widening the single-
value filter to multi-value via repeated flag using
multiFlags infrastructure from ADR-0183; mirror of
ADR-0207's multi-value pattern on diff-timeline;
closes the within-family asymmetry where retention
history's --actor-id-not stayed single-value while
diff-timeline's went multi-value; real cohort-
exclusion use cases — service-account allowlist audit
(exclude N known SAs in one command), self-exclude
reviewer cohort during incidents (exclude my actor +
on-call + SRE in one command), compliance non-
automation filter (regulatory human review excluding
3-5 automation SAs), cohort negative attestation
(every mutation NOT made by platform-admin cohort);
breaking adapter rename ListOptOutHistoryInput
.actorIdNot?: string → actorIdsNot?: ReadonlyArray
<string> session-recent code from ADR-0206 with no
external consumers contained scope matches ADR-0199
+ ADR-0207 one-shot break precedent; SQL changes
from single-value (h.actor_id IS NULL OR h.actor_id
!= $N) to multi-value (h.actor_id IS NULL OR
h.actor_id NOT IN ($N1, $N2, ...)); single-element
NOT IN ($N) is PG-equivalent to = $N so no
observable behavior change for single-value callers;
empty array treated as filter-not-set matching
ADR-0199/0207 convention operators may legitimately
compute empty list at call site; explicit IS NULL OR
prefix preserved — PG NOT IN returns NULL when
actor_id IS NULL silently filtering system events
OUT (wrong intent); duplicates not deduplicated at
adapter PG handles fine via NOT IN semantic matches
ADR-0207 stance; CLI reads via getMultiFlag instead
of getStringFlag; JSON envelope renamed actorIdNot
→ actorIdsNot string[] | null matching ADR-0207
array-or-null canonical multi-value shape; within-
surface asymmetry documented — retention history's
positive --actor-id stays single-value while negative
--actor-id-not now multi-value documented as future Q
deferred to keep milestone scope focused on closing
ADR-0206 Q1 specifically; asymmetry acceptable
because positive + negative semantically distinct
dimensions (negative path benefits more from multi-
value since "exclude these N noise actors" more
common than "include exactly these N specific
actors"), diff-timeline has both multi-value for
operators wanting multi-value positive, asymmetry
operator-visible via helpText + JSON envelope shape;
adapter test block rewritten + expanded from 8 to 11
tests under renamed describe block — single-element
NOT IN ($1), multi-element NOT IN ($1, $2), omits
clause when not set, empty array filter-not-set
(NEW), includes system events IS NULL prefix
preserved, composes with tenantId multi-actor +
tenant, composes with actorId positive + negative,
composes with joinActor LEFT JOIN + NOT IN,
composes with all filter dimensions full param array,
returns rows excluding listed actors, duplicate
actorIdsNot values produce duplicate placeholders
(NEW) PG dedupes via NOT IN; plus one cross-reference
fix in system-only describe block; CLI test block
rewritten + expanded from 6 to 8 tests under renamed
describe block — threads single-element array when
set once, threads multi-element array when repeated
(NEW), omits when not set backward compat, composes
with --actor-id positive + negative both threaded,
composes with --tenant + --with-actor-names + multi
--actor-id-not, JSON envelope echoes single-element
array, JSON envelope echoes multi-element array
(NEW), JSON envelope actorIdsNot=null when not set;
test count 8,955 → 8,960 (+5 net: adapter +3, CLI
+2); cli.ts helpText updated from [--actor-id-not
<uuid>] to [--actor-id-not <uuid> ...] indicating
repeatable + description extended explaining
repeatable + OR-semantic NOT IN + system-events-
included semantic; rejected alternatives — two-field
adapter surface (actorIdNot string + actorIdsNot
array permanent two-field invites operator confusion;
one-shot rename cleaner matches ADR-0199),
--actor-id-not <a,b,c> comma-separated (fragile with
shell-quoted UUIDs containing punctuation),
--actor-id-not-list <file.txt> (adds file-reading
code path defer), keep single-value document jq-
workaround (fails LIMIT + pagination correctness),
widen --actor-id to multi-value in same milestone
(would close ADR-0186 future Q + restore within-
surface symmetry BUT exceeds requested scope
documented as future Q), substrate-side dedup of
duplicates (PG handles fine), CLI-side UUID
validation per occurrence (PG errors clearer matches
deferred decision), pipe-separated alternative
(shell-quoting concerns), ENV-style flag names
(verbose), adapter-side empty-array rejection (
operators may legitimately compute empty list
operator-friendly). The --actor-id-not family is now
multi-value uniformly across both filter surfaces —
retention history (this milestone) + retention diff-
timeline (ADR-0207) — plus single-value expectation
check on the cross-event diff surface (retention
diff-history from ADR-0205); operators get cohort-
exclusion ergonomics with surface-appropriate
semantics under one consistent flag name. The
retention CLI now has 18 actions with multi-value
--actor-id-not support on the two list-style audit-
log filter surfaces; widening --actor-id to multi-
value on retention history (closes ADR-0186 future
Q) is the natural symmetric follow-up milestone).

(`crossengin retention history --actor-id <uuid>`
repeatable for multi-value OR-semantic positive
filter — closes ADR-0186 Q1 by widening single-value
--actor-id to multi-value via repeated flag using

symmetry that ADR-0210 explicitly documented as a
future Q — both positive --actor-id and negative
--actor-id-not are now multi-value on retention
history matching diff-timeline's symmetric design
(ADR-0199 positive multi + ADR-0207 negative multi);
real cohort-inclusion use cases — multi-actor cohort
audit (auditor + reviewer + approver triad), multi-
admin attestation ("every mutation by ANY of these 4
admins"), multi-SA observability (3 migration SAs
simultaneously), composed positive cohort + exclusion
("any of these N expected actors EXCEPT these M noise
actors"); breaking adapter rename
ListOptOutHistoryInput.actorId?: string → actorIds?:
ReadonlyArray<string> session-recent code from
ADR-0186 with no external consumers contained scope
matches ADR-0199 + ADR-0210 one-shot break precedent;
SQL changes from single-value h.actor_id = $N to
multi-value h.actor_id IN ($N1, $N2, ...); single-
element IN ($N) is PG-equivalent to = $N no
observable behavior change for single-value callers;
empty array treated as filter-not-set matching
ADR-0199/0207/0210 convention; no IS NULL OR prefix
on positive filter unlike negative — operators
wanting "match these actors" do NOT mean "and also
include all system events"; PG IN returns NULL for
null actor_id correctly filtered out; operators
wanting mixed cohort + system compose with --no-system
/ --system-only from ADR-0209 explicitly (mixed mode
deferred); CLI reads via getMultiFlag instead of
getStringFlag; JSON envelope renamed actorId →
actorIds string[] | null matching ADR-0210 array-or-
null canonical shape; adapter test block rewritten +
expanded from 7 to 11 tests under renamed describe
block — single-element IN ($1), multi-element IN
($1, $2), omits clause when not set, empty array
filter-not-set (NEW), composes with tenantId multi-
actor + tenant, composes with joinActor LEFT JOIN +
IN, composes with actorIdsNot positive + negative
both threaded (NEW), composes with all filter
dimensions full param array, returns rows matching
actors, returns empty when no rows match, duplicate
actorIds values produce duplicate placeholders (NEW)
PG dedupes via IN; plus updates to two cross-
reference tests in before-id + range describe blocks
and one in actor-not block; CLI test block rewritten
+ expanded from 7 to 9 tests under renamed describe
block — threads single-element array when set once,
threads multi-element array when --actor-id repeated
(NEW), omits when not set backward compat, composes
with --tenant + multi --actor-id + --kind, composes
with --with-actor-names multi actor + LEFT JOIN,
JSON envelope echoes single-element array, JSON
envelope echoes multi-element array (NEW), JSON
envelope actorIds=null when not set, human-format
empty-result message preserved; test count 8,960 →
8,966 (+6 net: adapter +4, CLI +2); cli.ts helpText
updated from [--actor-id <uuid>] to [--actor-id
<uuid> ...] indicating repeatable + description
extended explaining repeatable + OR-semantic IN;
rejected alternatives — ship in same milestone as
ADR-0210 (exceeded user scope for prior milestone;
shipping as follow-up respects per-request scope
discipline), two-field adapter surface (permanent
invites operator confusion one-shot rename cleaner),
--actor-id <a,b,c> comma-separated (fragile),
keep single-value document asymmetry as permanent
(ignoring entrenches asymmetry restoring symmetry
operator-friendly), include null actor_id in IN-list
match (semantic unclear keep IN pure operators
compose with system flags explicitly), @file.txt
substitution defer, CLI-side dedup of duplicates
(PG handles fine), --actor-name via meta.users JOIN
defer; future Qs — --actor-id @file.txt for very
large cohorts, CLI-side UUID validation per
occurrence, substrate-side dedup, composite index
for scale, --actor-name JOIN, PG IN-list chunking
for 1K+ actors, mixed cohort + system mode (actor_id
IN OR IS NULL), apply multi-value widening to
retention history's --kind defer since 4-value enum
makes multi less compelling. The retention history
surface's actor filter family is now symmetric multi-
value matching diff-timeline; diff-history stays
single-value because per-event-pair expectation check
has different semantic (multi-value tuple expectation
deferred to ADR-0205 Q1 / ADR-0203 Q2). The retention
CLI now has 18 actions with multi-value --actor-id +
--actor-id-not support on the two list-style audit-
log filter surfaces.
ADR-0212 covers M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.system-only
(`crossengin retention diff-history --system-only` /
`--no-system` actor-presence expectation check — closes
ADR-0205 Q3; diff-history expectation-check companion to
ADR-0209's substrate-side filter on retention history;
--system-only asserts BOTH events have actor_id IS NULL
(both system-authored); --no-system asserts NEITHER has
actor_id IS NULL (both have UUID actor); semantic differs
from ADR-0209's filter because diff-history takes 2 fixed
IDs not a list query — flag shifts from filter to
expectation check matching ADR-0198/0203/0205/0208 pattern
where the same flag becomes per-event-pair assertion;
mutually exclusive at CLI boundary both set returns exit
2 with explicit error before any PG query matching
ADR-0209 history pattern; composes with all existing
expectation flags --kind --kind-not --actor-id
--actor-id-not --with-actor-names without restriction;
reuses ActorPresenceFilter discriminated union from
ADR-0209 same type same mutual-exclusivity same JSON
envelope echo shape; DiffHistoryEntriesInput gains
optional actorPresence?: ActorPresenceFilter field; new
expectation check block positioned after existing
actorIdNot check (actor-dimension group); system_only
throws naming offending side(s) with actual actor_id
value ("but A is '<uuid>'") mirroring ADR-0203 positive
--actor-id rendering — operators need to see WHO authored
the event if it wasn't supposed to have a human actor;
no_system throws naming side(s) as <system> placeholder
("but A is <system>" / "both A and B are <system>")
mirroring ADR-0205 negative --actor-id-not rendering —
operators already know which side they expected to be
human naming the sentinel suffices; different rendering
reflects different information needs (positive needs to
show unexpected UUID; negative just names the side since
<system> is the excluded value); check ordering —
eventKind → eventKindNot → actorId → actorIdNot →
actorPresence (kind-dimension first actor-dimension
second with specific-actor before presence); use cases
unblocked — system-only audit pair verification (assert
both events are scheduled sweep events), human-only
forensic comparison (assert neither is system before
drawing responsibility conclusions), compliance
attestation gating (--no-system + --kind opt_out_set),
migration verification (--system-only + --kind
retention_set); CLI threads systemOnlyFlag + noSystemFlag
via getBooleanFlag, mutual-exclusivity check, translate
to actorPresence single value, thread to adapter, echo
systemOnly + noSystem booleans in JSON envelope matching
ADR-0209 history boolean-echo convention; composition
with --actor-id / --actor-id-not — --system-only +
--actor-id contradictory at data layer (first check
fires natural first-fail ordering), --system-only +
--actor-id-not redundant but valid, --no-system +
--actor-id canonical "both must be human X" idiom,
--no-system + --actor-id-not canonical "both must be
human excluding X" idiom; why expectation check not
filter — diff-history takes 2 fixed IDs PG-side WHERE
would silently return zero rows on mismatch
indistinguishable from "IDs don't exist" adapter throw
with clear message naming offending side(s) is loud and
clear matches ADR-0198/0203/0205/0208 precedent;
mismatch exits 1 (runtime path) CLI misuse mutual-
exclusivity exits 2; 10 new adapter tests in a new
"PostgresTraceRetention.diffHistoryEntries actorPresence
expectation check" describe block — system_only accepts
when both null, throws naming A when A has UUID with
actual UUID in error, throws naming B when B has UUID,
throws naming both sides when neither is system,
no_system accepts when both have UUID actor, throws when
A is <system> explicit rendering, throws when B is
<system>, throws naming both when both are <system>,
omits check when actorPresence not set backward compat,
composes with --kind expectation check (both pass when
actor + kind expectations met); 9 new CLI tests in a
new "runRetention diff-history --system-only /
--no-system" describe block — exit 2 when both flags
set with explicit error, threads actorPresence='system_
only' when --system-only set, threads actorPresence=
'no_system' when --no-system set, omits actorPresence
when neither flag set backward compat, composes with
--actor-id-not + --no-system both threaded
independently, adapter expectation error propagates exit
1 with explicit error including actual UUID, JSON
envelope echoes systemOnly=true + noSystem=false when
--system-only set, JSON envelope echoes noSystem=true +
systemOnly=false when --no-system set, JSON envelope
both false when neither set; test count 8,966 → 8,985
(+19: adapter +10, CLI +9); cli.ts helpText extended
for retention diff-history usage line with [--system-
only | --no-system] notation indicating mutual
exclusivity + 4-line description added explaining
expectation-check semantic + mutual exclusivity;
rejected alternatives — two separate boolean fields on
adapter (allows invalid both-true state; discriminated
union makes invalid unrepresentable), --actor-presence
single flag with enum value (operators remember enum
vocab vs natural boolean flags), --system/--human flag
names ("human" misleading SAs not human), --actor-id
null sentinel (overloads UUID semantic), CLI-side
--actor-id + --system-only contradiction error (would
block valid edge cases natural first-check-fires
outcome), substrate-side both-flag mutual-exclusivity
in adapter (type union makes invalid unrepresentable),
per-side --system-only-a/--system-only-b (overkill v1
defer), render mismatch as fieldDiff (actor presence
isn't field in next_state JSONB throwing loud and
clear), PG WHERE clause validation (would silently
return zero rows indistinguishable from missing IDs),
default --no-system silent behavior change backward-
incompatible. The retention diff-history surface now
has FIVE expectation-check flag families forming a 3×2
+ 1 matrix — kind dimension (--kind ADR-0198 +
--kind-not ADR-0208) + specific actor_id dimension
(--actor-id ADR-0203 + --actor-id-not ADR-0205) +
system actor presence dimension (--system-only +
--no-system from this milestone) + actor display
(--with-actor-names ADR-0204) — operators get full
positive + negative expectation gates across kind +
specific-actor + system-presence dimensions with
consistent error-message shape (positive: "but A is
'Y'"; negative: "but A matches" / "but A is <system>")
and uniform exit-code semantics (CLI misuse exit 2 on
mutual exclusivity; runtime exit 1 on adapter-level
expectation violation). The --system-only / --no-system
family is now 2-of-3 surfaces shipped: retention
history (substrate-side filter from ADR-0209); retention
diff-history (expectation check from this milestone);
retention diff-timeline (substrate-side filter across
all 3 paths from ADR-0207 Q3 + ADR-0193 future Q
deferred). The retention CLI now has 18 actions with
--system-only / --no-system support on both retention
history + retention diff-history surfaces).

/ --actor-id-not — --system-only + --actor-id is

covering pair-wise/N-way/cross-table for both
system_only + no_system + omits when not set +
composes with all dimensions + no params for IS NULL
clauses + LIMIT param position; 10 new CLI tests
covering exit 2 mutual exclusivity + pair-wise/N-way/
cross-table threading + composes with --actor-id-not +
JSON envelope echoes on all 3 envelope shapes; test
count 8,985 → 9,004 (+19: adapter +9, CLI +10);
cli.ts helpText extended on BOTH diff-timeline usage
lines with [--system-only | --no-system] notation +
descriptions updated explaining IS NULL / IS NOT NULL
filter semantic + mutual exclusivity; rejected
alternatives — two separate boolean fields on adapter
(allows invalid both-true; discriminated union makes
unrepresentable), single-path delivery (would force
N-way + cross-table operators to jq-filter post-fetch
breaking LIMIT correctness; symmetric coverage matches
ADR-0193/0194/0199/0200/0202/0207), --actor-presence
single enum flag (operators remember enum vocab),
--system/--human names ("human" misleading), --actor-id
null sentinel overload, CLI-side contradictory-
combination preempting (operators may script both flags
substrate returns empty natural outcome), partial PG
index in this milestone premature, substrate-side
mutual exclusivity in adapter (type union makes
unrepresentable CLI handles), apply actorPresence to
retention diff different surface different semantic
defer, tri-state --actor-presence both option
operator-confusing default. The --system-only /
--no-system family is now COMPLETE across all 3
retention surfaces — retention history (substrate-side
filter single path ADR-0209) + retention diff-history
(expectation check 2 fixed IDs ADR-0212) + retention
diff-timeline (substrate-side filter 3 dispatch paths
from this milestone). Operators get system-vs-human
ergonomics on every retention CLI surface with
surface-appropriate semantics (filter on list-style
queries; expectation check on cross-event diff). The
retention CLI now has 18 actions with comprehensive
multi-flag coverage — 4 actor-related filter families
(--actor-id, --actor-id-not, --system-only,
--no-system) on list-style surfaces all multi-value or

plus --with-actor-names).
ADR-0214 covers M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.kind-not.multi
(`crossengin retention diff-history --kind-not` repeatable
for multi-value OR-semantic exclusion expectation check —
closes ADR-0208 Q1; widens single-value --kind-not to
multi-value via repeated flag using multiFlags from
ADR-0183; operator declares "I expect NEITHER event to
have any of these N event_kinds" and adapter throws on
match with side(s) named; mirror of ADR-0210 history
actor-id-not multi-value + ADR-0207 diff-timeline actor-
id-not multi-value + ADR-0200 diff-timeline multi-kind —
same flag-repetition pattern + same one-shot breaking
rename + same per-occurrence validation against 4-value
enum + same empty-array-as-filter-not-set convention;
real cohort-exclusion use cases — workflow audit
excluding maintenance kinds (assert NEITHER event is
policy_deleted OR retention_set), anti-automation pair
verification, CI gate with kind-list exclusion, cohort-
aware forensic comparison composing with --system-only
from ADR-0212; breaking adapter rename
DiffHistoryEntriesInput.eventKindNot?:
OptOutHistoryEventKind → eventKindsNot?: ReadonlyArray
<OptOutHistoryEventKind> session-recent code from
ADR-0208 with no external consumers contained scope
matches ADR-0199/0207/0210/0211 one-shot break
precedent (6 consecutive single→multi renames make this
mechanical); adapter rewritten with Set-based O(1)
membership lookup + always-list error format
`event_kind in ['X']` for single-value + `event_kind
in ['X', 'Y']` for multi-value (single-value error
breaks from ADR-0208's `event_kind 'X'` shape
acceptable since field rename is itself breaking;
consistent rendering across single + multi reads
better than separate special-case formats); CLI reads
via getMultiFlag instead of getStringFlag with per-
occurrence validation via isOptOutHistoryEventKind
loop matching ADR-0200 pattern — exits 2 on FIRST
invalid value with that value named (operators reading
error know exactly which --kind-not occurrence
failed); JSON envelope renamed kindNot: string | null
→ kindsNot: string[] | null matching ADR-0207/0210/
0211 array-or-null canonical multi-value envelope;
within-action asymmetry documented — positive --kind
stays single-value while negative --kind-not now
multi-value; documented as future Q (widen --kind to
multi-value tuple expectation closes ADR-0198 Q2 +
restores within-action symmetry); deferred to keep
milestone scope focused on closing ADR-0208 Q1
specifically; asymmetry acceptable because positive +
negative semantically distinct dimensions (negative
path benefits more from multi-value since "exclude
these N noise kinds" is more common than "include
exactly one of these N specific kinds"), the 4-value
enum makes positive less compelling (operators wanting
"any of these 3 kinds" can equivalently use "neither
of remaining 1 kind" via --kind-not — negative is
strict superset); adapter test block rewritten +
expanded from 8 to 12 tests including new multi-value
accept + multi-value throws with multi-value error
format + empty-array filter-not-set + new error format
single-value coverage; CLI test block rewritten +
expanded from 7 to 10 tests including new FIRST-
invalid-occurrence exit 2 + new multi-element threading
+ new multi-element JSON envelope echo; test count
9,004 → 9,011 (+7 net: adapter +4, CLI +3); cli.ts
helpText updated from [--kind-not <event-kind>] to
[--kind-not <event-kind> ...] indicating repeatable +
description extended explaining repeatable + NEITHER
event has any of listed kinds + match-by-either-side
semantic; rejected alternatives — two-field adapter
(permanent invites confusion one-shot rename cleaner),
--kind-not <a>|<b>|<c> pipe-separated (fragile shell-
quoting), --kind-not <a>,<b>,<c> comma-separated
(fragile defer), keep single-value document jq-
workaround (fails exit-code-semantic argument), ship
in same milestone as --kind multi-value tuple (exceeds
user scope documented as future Q), keep single-value
error message format (inconsistent across single/
multi-value; always-list predictable), PG-side WHERE
NOT IN (diff-history takes 2 fixed IDs not list query),
substrate-side dedup (Set handles fine matches
ADR-0207/0210/0211), auto-validate at adapter (CLI
handles validation at boundary), separate --kind-not-
any flag (adds CLI surface with two near-identical
names); future Qs — widen --kind to multi-value tuple
expectation closes ADR-0198 Q2 + restores within-
action symmetry natural follow-up, --kind-not @file
defer since 4-value enum bounded, --kind-not filter
on retention history pairs with ADR-0206 Q4 defer,
--kind-not filter on diff-timeline across all 3 paths
pairs with ADR-0207 Q4 defer, CLI-side dedup defer,
semantic-shape exclusion --exclude-write-mutations
shorthand operator-policy defer. The retention diff-
history surface uniquely keeps single-value semantics
on most flags because expectation-check has different
operator ergonomics than list-query filter — operators
making per-event assertions usually have specific
values in mind not cohorts. The negative kind-not
filter is the first multi-value expectation check on
diff-history; the symmetric positive --kind multi-
value is the natural follow-up. The retention CLI now
has 18 actions with comprehensive multi-flag coverage
spanning the actor + kind dimensions across all 3
surfaces with surface-appropriate semantics).

(per-side asymmetric expectation checks on retention
diff-history — closes ADR-0198 Q1 + ADR-0203 Q1 +

future Qs in one milestone; 5th future Q ADR-0212 Q2

closes ADR-0205 Q2; ADDITIVE not breaking rename
DiffHistoryEntriesInput gains 8 new optional fields
preserving the existing 5 global fields per-side flags
are semantically DIFFERENT operations from global flags
not just renames; check ordering global before per-side
within each pair (global symmetric fires first when

existing global counterparts; JSON envelope gains 8
new fields matching CLI flag names; composition with
global flags fires global first natural ordering;
real asymmetric use cases — forensic state-transition
verification A=deletion B=rebuild, per-side actor
attribution A=Alice B=Bob, per-side exclusion different
actors per side, per-side multi-value kind exclusion,
hybrid global + per-side belt-and-suspenders,
maximum-discipline forensic assertion combining all
per-side variants; 17 new adapter tests + 17 new CLI
tests = 34 total; test count 9,011 → 9,045; rejected
alternatives — combine into global flags via A/B
comma-separated semantic-overload breaking change,
positional A/B semantics unusual flag form, substrate-
side dedup of contradictory global + per-side
operator may script CLI doesn't pre-empt, ship --system-
only-a/-b in this milestone boolean-pair × per-side
deserves own design defer, --actor-id-not-a vs
--actor-id-a-not naming consistency adopted, single
combined --side modifier flag verbose + non-composable,
--a-kind/--b-kind prefix form non-canonical, per-side
multi-value --actor-id-a repeatable separate design
defer, adapter-side global + per-side mutual exclusivity
check operator intent ambiguous CLI passes both
adapter runs both, Map-based adapter input verbose
flat fields match CLI flag names; future Qs — --system-
only-a/-b + --no-system-a/-b boolean-pair × per-side
defer closes ADR-0212 Q2, per-side multi-value
--actor-id-a repeatable for tuple expectation defer,
JSON envelope field name distinction between operator-
input vs result diff values defer rename, apply per-
side to retention diff-timeline N/A list-style not per-
event-pair, --kind-a accepts multi-value tuple defer,
combined per-side + system actor presence --system-
only-a + --no-system-b defer. The retention diff-
history surface now has 13 expectation-check flag
families forming a 4×3 matrix — 4 dimensions
(event_kind positive, event_kind negative, actor_id
positive, actor_id negative) × 3 modes (global
symmetric, per-side A, per-side B) plus the actor-
presence pair --system-only/--no-system (global only);
operators have unprecedented control over per-event-
pair forensic assertions with symmetric + asymmetric
expectations across kind + actor + system-presence
dimensions in a single command. The retention CLI now
has 18 actions with the most comprehensive multi-flag
coverage in the codebase — operators can express
virtually any state-transition assertion in a single
command. The natural follow-up milestones — --system-
only-a/-b + --no-system-a/-b per-side boolean-pair
(ADR-0212 Q2), multi-value tuple expectations on per-
side variants (ADR-0198 Q2 / ADR-0203 Q2 family)).
ADR-0216 covers M6.7.zz.tenant.opt-out.cli.diff-history.per-side.system-only
(per-side --system-only/--no-system actor-presence
expectation check on retention diff-history — closes

pattern; check ordering AFTER same-dimension contradiction
BEFORE adapter call; error format `--system-only{+side} +
--actor-id{+side} — system-only requires actor_id IS NULL but

construction`; asymmetric treatment --no-system + --actor-id
valid redundant non-null vs --system-only + --actor-id
contradiction definite-empty; cross-side --system-only-a +

"plan echoes all filters" test updated from --system-only to
--no-system; 9 new CLI tests test count 9,220 → 9,229;
rejected — detect --no-system + --actor-id redundancy as
warning adds noise on valid composition, auto-resolve by
dropping flag operator intent ambiguous, --allow-contradictory-
semantics adds complexity, adapter-side detection wrong layer,

intentional asymmetric per ADR-0215, generic constraint solver
framework premature abstraction, defer to future semantic

+ per-side cross-flag contradictions adapter ordering handles
defer, detection across surfaces uniformly inherits pattern,
CLI hint about valid alternatives defer operator-UX polish,
JSON output for contradiction errors defer operator-UX polish.

— same-dimension set-intersection (ADR-0226) + cross-
dimensional semantic (ADR-0230); together they catch the most
common contradictory-flag patterns operators encounter).
